title: DECISION: No silent degradation — VectorStore-only search path, SemanticIndex fallback removed
description:
## Decision

VectorStore is the ONLY search path. The SemanticIndex fallback and JSON cache deserialization (load_with_lock, 1.1GB HashMap) are removed from the query flow entirely. On any VectorStore failure (load/build/search/save), propagate a detailed error message to the user — never silently degrade to the slow path.

## Rationale

Silent fallback to SemanticIndex (4-5s cold JSON load + per-query ONNX model reload) makes the user believe that IS the tool's performance. They never discover the fast path failed, never report the bug, and never get it fixed. The only acceptable fallback is a clear, actionable error message that tells the user what broke and how to fix it.

A tool should run at 100% performance or tell you why it can't. Running in degraded mode without the user's knowledge is a UX anti-pattern — it hides bugs behind mediocre-but-functional behavior.

## Alternatives Considered

- **Keep SemanticIndex fallback (current design)**: Rejected — hides VectorStore failures from users, makes performance bugs invisible, and preserves the slow JSON cache load path that m01/ac0 exist to eliminate.
- **Fallback with a visible warning**: Rejected — warnings are ignored by users, especially in CLI tool output. The tool still "works" so the warning is treated as noise.
- **Fallback with opt-in flag (--allow-degraded)**: Rejected — over-engineered for the problem. If VectorStore is broken, fix VectorStore.

## Affects

- TLDR-zxb: Wire search_with_store into CLI/daemon/MCP (must implement error-only, no fallback)
- TLDR-m01: Rewire EmbeddingCache onto usearch Index (store_search.rs fallback code removed)
- store_search.rs: SemanticIndex import and fallback branch removed entirely
close_reason: Decision recorded and linked to zxb + m01. Enforced in zxb implementation.

title: DECISION: Incremental re-indexing lives in the daemon (RAM writable index), gated on warm path
description: Because view() is read-only (see storage-split decision), incremental updates (remove stale function vectors + add changed ones, instead of the full ~30min re-embed) REQUIRE a RAM-resident writable usearch Index. DECISION: that index lives in the DAEMON (TLDR-atc). The daemon holds the writable Index, watches file mtimes, applies per-function deltas, and periodically save()s to disk. Cold CLI invocations use read-only view() of the last saved file. CONSEQUENCE: incremental indexing is NOT a property of usearch alone — it is unlocked by usearch + the daemon warm path together. This elevates TLDR-atc from a latency win to the keystone for incremental indexing.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: Add daemon warm path for semantic search (no semantic route exists — every query is cold)
description: DISCOVERED 2026-06-01 via daemon route scan + tldr explain. tldr-daemon exposes 21 routes (ping/status/tree/structure/extract/imports/calls/impact/dead/importers/arch/cfg/dfg/slice/complexity/search[BM25]/context/smells/maintainability/secrets/vuln) and ZERO semantic/similar/embed routes. crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/semantic.rs has 0 daemon references. CONSEQUENCE: every 'tldr semantic'/'similar'/'embed' invocation runs COLD in a fresh process -> SemanticIndex::build -> EmbeddingCache::open -> load_with_lock (cache.rs:236) deserializes the full ~1.1GB cache each time (RSS ~8GB), reloads the 110MB ONNX model (~0.5s), then embeds the query (~0.2s). For warm/interactive use this is a BIGGER lever than usearch: usearch view() removes the 4.3s load, but a daemon warm path removes the load AND the 0.5s model load AND keeps the index resident -> ac0 estimated ~230ms warm. SCOPE: add /semantic (+/similar,/embed) daemon route + handler that holds a warm SemanticIndex (mmap'd vectors once usearch lands) + a warm Embedder (model loaded once) in DaemonState, keyed by project+model+mtimes like other warm caches. Pairs with TLDR-ac0 (usearch storage) and TLDR-h27/y0q (CLI correctness).
acceptance_criteria: tldr semantic/similar/embed route through the daemon when running; warm query latency ~200-300ms (no per-query 1.1GB load, no per-query model reload); cold fallback preserved when daemon absent; results identical to cold path.
notes:
UNLOCKS INCREMENTAL INDEXING (research 2026-06-01): usearch view()'d mmap index is READ-ONLY (issue#97 — cannot add to disk). Incremental updates (remove stale keys + add changed-function vectors, avoiding the full ~30min re-embed) REQUIRE a RAM-resident writable index. The daemon warm path is exactly that home: hold the writable usearch Index in DaemonState, apply deltas on file-mtime change, periodically save(). So this P0 is not just a latency win — it's the prerequisite for incremental re-indexing. Cold CLI keeps using read-only view().
2026-06-02 STATUS: warm path + root-relative cache keys committed (401a7ac); off-executor spawn_blocking committed (7199f03). REMAINING: live stop-mid-build validation (was pending the cache being free). The spawn_blocking follow-up bug (handle_notify on async thread) is TLDR-qr9, now folded into TLDR-ac0.2 (in-daemon watcher → internal channel). Consider closing atc once the live stop-mid-build check is done.
close_reason: Warm path ships VectorStore — load_or_build_store is near-instant on fresh store. Daemon holds resident (Model, VectorStore). Remaining improvements (incremental delta, watcher, concurrent reads) are separate issues (t8f, ac0.2, ac0.1).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: POST-ZXB (2026-06-02): The daemon now holds a resident VectorStore (Mutex<Option<(Model, VectorStore)>>) instead of SemanticIndex. Warm command uses load_or_build_store — near-instant when store is fresh on disk. The original atc acceptance ("warm query latency ~200-300ms, no per-query 1.1GB load, no per-query model reload") is MET for the common case (store exists, nothing changed). The "cold fallback preserved when daemon absent" criterion changes per TLDR-lx7: no fallback, errors propagate. Consider closing atc — the warm path works, remaining improvements (incremental delta, watcher) are separate issues (t8f, ac0.2).

title: Default 0.50 similarity threshold miscalibrated for query-prefix regime (hides correct results)
description: DISCOVERED while dogfooding tldr semantic on our own codebase (TLDR-blm work). The Arctic query prefix (TLDR-dlk, default-ON) improves RANKING decisively (eval recall@5 0.071->0.857) but LOWERS absolute cosine scores, because the asymmetric prefix puts query embeddings in a different region than raw-code passages (query.passage cosine < passage.passage cosine). The user-facing 'tldr semantic' applies a fixed default --threshold 0.50. Result: the CORRECT answer for 'cosine similarity between two embedding vectors' is cosine_similarity() but it scores 0.46 -> filtered out -> CLI prints 'No matches found above threshold'. So the feature looks BROKEN to a user despite the recall win. The eval harness bypasses the threshold (pure ranking) which is why it reported 0.857. FIX OPTIONS: (a) lower/remove the default threshold and rely on top-k ranking; (b) recalibrate threshold for the prefixed-query score distribution; (c) make threshold prefix-aware. Repro: tldr semantic 'cosine similarity between two embedding vectors' crates/tldr-core/src/semantic --top 3  (default threshold -> no matches; --threshold 0.0 -> cosine_similarity appears at rank 3, score 0.46).
acceptance_criteria: tldr semantic with default settings surfaces the correct top-ranked result for the gold queries (not filtered by threshold); eval-measured ranking and CLI-visible results agree.
notes: Child of TLDR-blm epic. Surfaced by the prefix verdict; a user-facing gap between eval-measured ranking (good) and CLI-visible results (threshold-suppressed).
close_reason: Default semantic threshold 0.5->0.0 (rank top-N, no cutoff) so the prefix-regime scores (~0.46) are no longer hidden; also fixed the formatter's hardcoded 0.50 display to show the actual threshold. PROVEN: 'tldr semantic <q>' with default settings now returns cosine_similarity instead of 'No matches'. semantic.rs.

title: Measure post-F1 warm memory peak (closes TLDR-9ae's deferred verification; ADR-6 decision input)
description:
TLDR-9ae closed with 'memory-delta measurement queued' — this is that queue entry. F1 (drop to_value DOM from warm inserts, commit 100f465) is shipped but its memory effect is UNMEASURED: the callgraph-phase plateau was 11.4GB PRE-F1; post-F1 number unknown.

PROTOCOL (cheap now — warm callgraph rebuild is ~22s after the Gate-1 campaign, was 3.5min): restart daemon with current binary (store fresh on disk, embedding cache warm -> only callgraph rebuilds) -> poll status Memory line through the warm -> record peak + settled. Compare vs 11.4GB pre-F1 plateau.

NOTE: requires starting a resident daemon (user authorization point per 2026-06-05 session).

WHY IT MATTERS: one of exactly two missing inputs to the Path A/B call in ADR-6 (TLDR-7mp) — if F1 already took most of the callgraph transient, the chunk store's memory case shrinks toward zero and Path A wins. Deeper attribution (if the number is still big) = TLDR-335's full protocol.

title: Hook-based pre-injection mode: push context into agent prompt before the model thinks
description:
From GrapeRoot competitive analysis (TLDR-2o4), finding #1 — the big one.

PROBLEM: tldr is entirely PULL — the agent must decide to call tldr (MCP/CLI), formulate a query, and pay a tool-call round-trip per lookup. An agent that doesn't reach for tldr gets zero value. GrapeRoot PUSHES ~4k-token structured context into the prompt in ~150ms via Claude Code hooks (UserPromptSubmit / SessionStart), zero tool calls. They ran our exact architecture (MCP graph tools) first and pivoted away after measuring per-call protocol overhead compounding across turns.

PROPOSAL: a hook-based pre-injection mode on top of the existing daemon. The daemon already has the warm Salsa caches and a fast answer path. Sketch:
- 'tldr hooks install' writes a UserPromptSubmit (and/or SessionStart) hook into Claude Code settings
- hook hits the daemon with the user prompt, daemon ranks relevant files/symbols (existing search + semantic when available), packs a compact structured summary (signatures, call edges, maybe inline hot-function bodies) under a token budget
- inject as additionalContext; degrade gracefully (empty output) if daemon down or project unindexed
- latency budget: must answer in low hundreds of ms or skip — never block the prompt

OPEN QUESTIONS: token budget (GrapeRoot uses ~4k; we should make it configurable), ranking quality from a raw user prompt (modality gap applies — our Arctic prefix work is an advantage here), how this composes with tldr-mcp (pre-injection for turn-1 context, MCP for deep on-demand queries is a plausible split).

title: (EPIC) Phase-1 ship readiness: quality gates green
description: Phase 1 (daemon presence-liveness + watcher + warm pipeline) cannot be called ship-ready while known quality gates are red. Scope: the pre-existing test failures (54 tldr-cli + 1 tldr-core), the -q output-swallowing CLI bug, and the flaky MCP microbench. Exit criteria: cargo test green across all crates (or failures explicitly waived with rationale), clippy clean, no -q data loss. Sibling ship-blockers tracked in their own epics: TLDR-bpf (ignore contract), TLDR-k8s (22GB warm memory).

title: F1: remove serde_json::to_value triple-copy from warm cache inserts
description: daemon.rs:141-144 (calls) and 160-163 (structure): result → to_value DOM → insert(&val) where insert (salsa.rs:232) serializes AGAIN via to_vec. The Value DOM (5-15x JSON text size; every key a heap String) is pure waste — insert<T: Serialize> can take &result directly. One-line change per site + verify cache.get::<serde_json::Value> round-trips identically (to_vec(&result) and to_vec(&to_value(&result)) produce the same bytes for serde-compatible types). Expected: minus several GB off the plateau. Code-verified; does NOT need the Phase-A measurement to proceed.
notes: Code landed (100f465): 3 warm sites pass native result to insert; no to_value DOM. Memory verification (warm peak delta) deferred until the running cold build completes — daemon restart required.
close_reason: F1 landed in 100f465 (3 warm sites, no to_value DOM); functional verification via identical warm behavior + tests; memory-delta measurement queued now that the cold build completed

title: run Phase-A malloc attribution protocol on the 22GB callgraph plateau
description: Execute the step-by-step protocol in TLDR-k8s design notes §3: MallocStackLogging=1 daemon start → warm → malloc_history <pid> -allBySize mid-plateau (60-180s in) + vmmap + footprint snapshots. Also run the foreground-warm secondary harness (/usr/bin/time -l, daemon stopped — callgraph only, skips the daemon copy stack): the DELTA between daemon footprint and foreground max-RSS ≈ cost of the triple-copy (cause a) alone. Deliverable: top-10 allocation sites by bytes, each mapped to causes (a)-(d), recorded on TLDR-k8s. PRECONDITION: embedding cache warm (so embed phase is cache-hits and only the callgraph phase recomputes — ~5min total run).

title: (EPIC) Ignore contract: .tldrignore honored end-to-end — watcher, corpus, unified matcher
description: Phase-1 ship blocker. tldr auto-creates .tldrignore (warm.rs:240) but only callgraph scan + warm walks honor it. Confirmed live 2026-06-04: (a) watcher delta-embeds tldrignored paths (TLDR-1j2 repro: 3→4 vectors on vendored/ write, gitignored control unchanged); (b) initial semantic build over-indexes them (TLDR-1qv: 2 of 3 scratch-corpus vectors came from the ignored dir). Decided semantics: REINDEX-ONLY filtering (presence/liveness unchanged — cargo build in target/ still defers idle shutdown). Exit criteria: a path matched by .tldrignore or .gitignore never reaches the southbound pipeline (enqueue/salsa-invalidate/chunk/embed/index) from EITHER the watcher delta path or the full-build corpus enumeration, verified by the /tmp/tig repro + new unit tests.

title: warm-build 22GB memory blow-up: triple-copy insert + AST retention; debug plan + disk-backed chunk-invalidated salsa cache
description: Callgraph/structure warm phase transiently holds ~22GB phys_footprint (~900x of 24MB source) — verified NOT the embedding phase. Four code-verified causes: (a) triple-copy cache insert incl. removable serde_json::to_value DOM (daemon.rs:141-144,160-163 + salsa.rs:232), (b) build_indices_parallel retains source+AST+FileIR for all files (builder_v2.rs:638), (c) duplicated indices (builder_v2.rs:682-685), (d) no string interning. Design notes contain: full evidence, step-by-step MallocStackLogging/malloc_history debug protocol (run later), ranked fix plan F1-F4, and a design sketch for persisting the salsa cache to disk with PER-FILE CHUNK-BASED invalidation (replaces whole-project entries; kills restart recompute + invalidation cliff; watcher invalidate_by_input plumbing already exists).
design:
# Warm-build 22GB memory blow-up: root cause, debug plan, and persistent chunked salsa cache

## 1. WHAT IS THE ISSUE

`tldr warm` on tldr-code (~24MB source, 1570 files) drives the daemon to ~22GB
physical footprint — a ~900x input blow-up — during the CALLGRAPH/STRUCTURE phase
(first ~3.5min), NOT the semantic/embedding phase as originally assumed.

Evidence (2026-06-04, branch build, instrumented via status Memory + ps + vmmap):
- Warm-cache run: dead-flat 11.4GB RSS plateau for ~3.5min, then an 8GB cliff
  (11.3→3.4GB between consecutive 15s samples) at the phase boundary.
- Cold run: vmmap at ~3min (embedding NOT started): Physical footprint 22.0GB,
  of which MALLOC_LARGE = 21.8GB across only 317-375 regions (avg ~70MB/region =
  giant Vec/map doubling-reallocs). status Memory said 13.7GB (peak 16.0) at the
  same moment — the mach resident-size probe underreports because macOS compresses
  idle pages; phys_footprint is the honest metric.
- Flat-then-cliff = one big retained working set, dropped wholesale when the warm
  step's scope ends. Not a leak; a transient retention problem.

## 2. WHY 22GB — four compounding causes (code-verified)

(a) TRIPLE-COPY cache insert (crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/daemon.rs:141-144,
    repeated for structure at 160-163):
      result = build_project_call_graph(...)      // copy 1: native structs, whole project
      val = serde_json::to_value(&result)         // copy 2: JSON DOM, 5-15x JSON text size
                                                  //   (every key a heap String, every node
                                                  //    a tagged enum in a Map)
      cache.insert(key, &val, vec![])             // copy 3: serde_json::to_vec(&val)
                                                  //   inside insert (salsa.rs:232)
    ALL THREE coexist at the insert call. The Value DOM is PURE WASTE:
    insert<T: Serialize> can take &result directly. Cheapest big win.

(b) Parallel parse retains everything (tldr-core callgraph/builder_v2.rs:638):
    build_indices_parallel holds source text + tree-sitter AST (10-50x source)
    + FileIR per file concurrently; then ALL FileIRs are retained in CallGraphIR
    through the entire resolution phase.

(c) Admitted index duplication (builder_v2.rs:682-685): "We need our own copies
    because the IR's indices use a different format" — FuncIndex/ClassIndex built
    twice in different shapes, both alive simultaneously.

(d) No string interning: owned String file paths / names / signatures duplicated
    across tens of thousands of nodes, edges, indices — then duplicated AGAIN in
    the JSON DOM.

Blow-up arithmetic: 24MB source → ~2.5GB ASTs+FileIRs → x2 duplicated indices →
+ JSON DOM (5-15x) stacked on the native copy → + serialized bytes → ~22GB transient.

## 3. HOW TO DEBUG — STEP-BY-STEP (run later; ~20min total)

PRECONDITION: current cold build finished; embedding cache repopulated on disk, so
the embed phase will be cache-hits (~1min) and only the callgraph phase (~3.5min,
the phase under investigation) recomputes. Salsa cache is in-RAM only → daemon
restart guarantees the callgraph phase re-runs.

Step A1 — instrumented daemon:
    TLDR=~/Workspace/03-Parcadei-Ecosystem/tldr-code/target/release/tldr
    cd ~/Workspace/03-Parcadei-Ecosystem/tldr-code
    $TLDR daemon stop -p .
    MallocStackLogging=1 $TLDR daemon start -p .
    $TLDR daemon list          # → PID
    $TLDR warm .

Step A2 — sample mid-plateau (~60-180s after warm ack, while status shows
    busy: warm-build and Memory > 8GB):
    malloc_history <PID> -allBySize | head -60     # TOP ALLOCATION SITES W/ STACKS
    vmmap --summary <PID> | grep -E 'Physical footprint|MALLOC'
    footprint <PID>                                # categorized snapshot
    Record the top-10 sites by total bytes; map each to causes (a)-(d).

Step A3 — secondary single-number harness (callgraph phase ISOLATED, no daemon):
    $TLDR daemon stop -p .
    /usr/bin/time -l $TLDR warm .       # foreground warm = callgraph only,
                                        # no semantic step; prints max RSS
    NOTE: foreground path skips the daemon's to_value/insert copies, so
    (A3 max-RSS) vs (A2 footprint) DIFFERENCE ≈ cost of cause (a) alone.

Step A4 — after fixes land, re-run A1-A3; acceptance = peak phys_footprint
    during warm. Targets: <8GB after fix (a); <4GB stretch after (a)+(b).

## 4. FIX PLAN (ranked by effort/payoff)

F1 (trivial): delete the to_value intermediate at daemon.rs:141-144 and 160-163 —
    pass &result straight to cache.insert. Expected: minus several GB.
F2: stream/drop AST+source immediately after FileIR extraction inside
    build_indices_parallel; consider bounding rayon width for peak control.
F3: unify the duplicated indices (IR-internal vs step-9 copies).
F4: intern strings (ustr/lasso) for paths+names across nodes/edges/indices.

## 5. SALSA CACHE → DISK + CHUNK-BASED INVALIDATION (user proposal, design sketch)

Current state (salsa.rs — homegrown, not the salsa crate):
- In-RAM DashMap<QueryKey, CacheEntry{serialized JSON bytes}>; only stats are
  persisted (.tldr/cache/salsa_stats.json). Daemon restart loses ALL entries →
  full 22GB recompute every restart.
- Granularity = WHOLE-PROJECT result per query type ("calls" for the entire repo
  is ONE entry). invalidate_by_input(file_hash) dependency tracking exists, but
  since values are project-sized, ONE changed file nukes the whole entry and the
  next query pays full recompute (22GB again). This granularity mismatch is the
  structural root of both the memory spike and the recompute cliff.

Proposal — per-file chunked, disk-backed cache:
- Persist per-FILE chunks (FileIR or equivalent) keyed by (relative path,
  content hash), e.g. .tldr/cache/fileir/<path-hash>.bin — compact binary
  (bincode/rkyv; rkyv enables mmap zero-copy loads), NOT JSON.
- Project-level results (call graph, structure) become a COMPOSE step over
  chunks: load all fresh chunks + run cross-file resolution. The expensive
  per-file parse/extract is memoized; only the resolution pass recomputes.
- Invalidation becomes chunk-based: file change → recompute ONLY that file's
  chunk (mirrors the t8f semantic per-file delta), then re-run compose.
- Daemon restart: load chunks from disk (seconds, mmap) instead of re-parsing
  the world (3.5min + 22GB).

Benefits: kills restart recompute; kills whole-project invalidation cliff;
bounds peak memory (compose can stream chunks instead of retaining all ASTs).

Risks / open questions:
- Cross-file edge resolution genuinely needs a global pass — measure compose
  cost alone (expected: small fraction of parse cost, but verify).
- Format versioning: schema_version field in chunk header; mismatch → recompute.
- Atomic write discipline: reuse the vector_store generation pattern
  (write-new-gen + commit marker) to avoid torn caches.
- Disk size: FileIR binary for 24MB source — estimate during implementation;
  likely tens-to-hundreds of MB, fine for a cache dir.
- Consistency with the semantic store freshness digest (TLDR-kkt) and with
  .tldrignore membership rules (TLDR-1qv) — chunk enumeration must use the same
  corpus walker or the two caches drift.
- The watcher's process_dirty_file already calls invalidate_by_input per file —
  the IPC/watcher plumbing for chunk-level invalidation ALREADY EXISTS; only the
  storage granularity and compose step are new.

## 6. RELATED
- TLDR-yll: RSS observation tooling (status Memory line) + max-RSS policy; the
  probe should report phys_footprint, not just resident (underreport observed).
- TLDR-1j2/TLDR-1qv: .tldrignore gaps; chunk enumeration must share corpus rules.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
EPIC STATE UPDATE (2026-06-05, post Gate-1 campaign — read with the ADR chain TLDR-cv9..ab6):

The epic's original framing ('22GB warm blow-up; fix = triple-copy removal + chunk-store') is now PARTLY SUPERSEDED by measurement:

1. TIME axis RESOLVED: the 3.5min warm rebuild was ~98% compose-phase defects, not parse cost. Fixed (5 rounds, output-identical): build 226s -> 3.1s, compose 223s -> 0.56s. A warm callgraph rebuild now costs ~22s end-to-end in the daemon path. Gate-1 PASSED — recompose-always stands; the chunk store is NO LONGER a time play (saves only ~2.7s parse).

2. MEMORY axis RE-ATTRIBUTED (ADR-7): the headline peak (27.0GB high-water, 4.5h cold build 2026-06-05) is the EMBED phase — owned by TLDR-3rh/yll, NOT this epic's callgraph work and NOT addressable by the chunk store. This epic's remaining memory scope = the callgraph-phase transient: 11.4GB measured PRE-F1; F1 (TLDR-9ae) shipped; post-F1 number = TLDR-myd (new child, P1). If TLDR-myd shows F1 took most of it, remaining children re-rank: TLDR-1in (F2-F4 structural) becomes the main lever, TLDR-335 (malloc attribution) the diagnostic if numbers stay high, TLDR-zde Path B becomes optional.

3. HYPOTHESES now open under this epic:
- H-mem: post-F1 callgraph plateau is well under 11.4GB (the to_value DOM was 5-15x JSON text). Test: TLDR-myd (~22s run).
- H-lang: python/TS compose dominated by the same per-call-site-scan defect class (TLDR-olg, related). If true, the fix pattern is established and cheap.
- H-27GB: 27.0 vs historical 22.7 is measurement improvement (getrusage high-water vs 15s polling), not regression — overnight diff touched zero semantic/ code. Falsifiable only by an instrumented main-branch cold build (~4.5h, night-run candidate, NOT scheduled).

Child status: TLDR-9ae CLOSED (F1 shipped; verification -> TLDR-myd). TLDR-zde design field rewritten to post-Gate-1 state. TLDR-335/1in/3rh/d3s unchanged.

title: watcher: honor .tldrignore in watch_decision (reindex-only) + close gitignored-delete passthrough gap
description: Watcher never consults .tldrignore: edits in tldrignored dirs trigger full southbound reindex (salsa invalidate + re-embed + index insert). Decided scope: filter in watch_decision BEFORE the exists()/is_corpus_file branch using scanner.rs Gitignore-based matcher loaded once in spawn_watcher; presence_decision UNCHANGED (reindex-only per user decision 2026-06-04). Also closes the deleted-gitignored-file passthrough. Full design + tests + verification plan in design notes.
design:
# Watcher: honor .tldrignore (+ close .gitignore delete-path gap) in watch_decision — REINDEX-ONLY

## Problem (verified 2026-06-04, explanation session)

The in-daemon watcher (crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/watcher.rs) never consults
`.tldrignore`. `watch_decision` (watcher.rs:74) filters via: Access drop → `.tldr/` +
store-dir prefix → `is_corpus_file` (tldr-core chunker.rs:312). `is_corpus_file` honors
`.gitignore` / DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_DIRS / generated sentinels but has ZERO `.tldrignore`
references (confirmed: no matches in chunker.rs or walker.rs).

Consequence: an edit to a source file in a tldrignored-but-not-gitignored dir flows
OS event → enqueue → process_dirty_file (daemon.rs:1359) → salsa invalidation →
re-chunk + re-embed + index insert. Southbound work on a path the user excluded.

Secondary gap in the SAME decision fn: a DELETED gitignored file falls through the
vanished-path passthrough branch (is_corpus_file can't judge gone paths) and gets
enqueued; apply_delta's store-side filter drops it, but only after a wasted southbound hop.

Irony: the CLI-foreground warm AUTO-CREATES `.tldrignore` (warm.rs:240-251) — the
product promises the contract, the watcher breaks it.

## Decided scope (user decision 2026-06-04)

- REINDEX-ONLY: ignored paths must never reach the southbound pipeline (no enqueue,
  no salsa invalidation, no chunk/embed/index work).
- presence_decision is UNCHANGED: ignored-dir writes still count as presence
  (preserves the deliberate TLDR-3w5 choice that `cargo build` writing to gitignored
  `target/` keeps the daemon alive).
- OS events cannot be suppressed (FSEvents has no subtree exclusion); the guarantee
  is filtered-before-channel.

## Design

1. Reuse the Gitignore-based matcher from tldr_core::callgraph::scanner::load_tldrignore
   (scanner.rs:529 — proper glob semantics via matched_path_or_any_parents). Do NOT use
   warm.rs's name-stem HashSet load_tldrignore (warm.rs:323) — crude component matching.
   load_tldrignore is currently private; export it (or add a small pub helper
   `is_tldrignored(root, path, is_dir) -> bool`). filter_tldrignored (scanner.rs:554)
   is already pub but batch-shaped, wrong shape for per-event checks.
2. In spawn_watcher (watcher.rs:125): load the `.tldrignore` matcher ONCE, plus a
   root-`.gitignore` matcher (same GitignoreBuilder pattern) to close the deleted-
   gitignored-file gap. Move/clone both into the debounce-handler closure.
3. In watch_decision: check `matched_path_or_any_parents(rel_path, is_dir)` for BOTH
   matchers BEFORE the `path.exists()` / `is_corpus_file` branch. Order matters:
   the check must run before the exists() branch so DELETES inside ignored dirs are
   also dropped — same delete-trap shape as the existing `.tldr/` prefix check
   (is_corpus_file always returns false for vanished paths). For vanished paths use
   is_dir=false; parent-dir patterns like `vendored/` still match via _or_any_parents.
4. presence_decision: NO change. The presence tap (watcher.rs:193) stays pre-filter.
5. Tests (watcher.rs tests module already has the pattern):
   - tldrignored existing file modify → !watch_decision, presence_decision still true
   - tldrignored DELETED file → !watch_decision (delete-trap variant)
   - gitignored DELETED file → !watch_decision (the secondary gap)
   - .tldrignore absent → behavior identical to today
   - glob pattern (`*.gen.py`) and nested dir pattern (`vendor/sub/`) respected

## Documented limitations (v1, do not solve)

- Matchers load once at daemon start; editing .gitignore/.tldrignore mid-session
  requires daemon restart. (Possible follow-up: watcher sees events on those two
  files → rebuild matchers.)
- Presence-side and root-matcher only: nested .gitignore files and git global
  excludes are NOT covered by the new explicit matchers (is_corpus_file still
  covers them for existing files; the new matcher only needs to catch deletes).

## Related but SEPARATE defect (filed independently)

enumerate_corpus_files (chunker.rs:417) — the single source of truth for what gets
embedded — also ignores .tldrignore, so the initial warm build over-indexes
tldrignored dirs (wasted embed minutes + RSS + polluted search results). Fixing the
watcher alone leaves the full-build path inconsistent with the delta path.

## Verification plan (repro already scripted in session)

Scratch project /tmp/tig with `vendored/` in .tldrignore; warm; create new .py under
vendored/ → BEFORE fix: status vector count increases (bug). AFTER fix: count stable,
Watcher presence age still resets (reindex-only semantics confirmed).

title: (EPIC) Presence-based daemon liveness — daemon dies only when the project is dormant
description: GOVERNING PHILOSOPHY (user, 2026-06-04; full text on TLDR-3w5): the idle question changes from 'is anyone talking to my socket?' to 'is anyone alive in this project?'. Liveness = ANY tldr CLI invocation (all ~64 surfaces) + raw watcher events (pre-corpus-filter; a cargo build is proof of life) + the daemon's own in-flight work. Idle shutdown only when no human, no agent, no file event, no internal work for idle_timeout. Accepted trade-off: long-build machines effectively never idle out (warm availability over memory thrift). Codex-reviewed 2026-06-04 (adversarial, file:line grounded): ordering A->C->B->D with MCP parity + memory escape hatch parallel after A. A=TLDR-3w5 (ActivityTracker), C=status observability, B=CLI-wide poke, D=async warm ack (TLDR-utj.7).

title: daemon idle-timeout kills in-progress builds/deltas — fix: presence-based ActivityTracker (cxa child A)
description: REPRO (live, 2026-06-04): user ran 'tldr daemon start' + 'tldr warm' on tldr-code (1565 files, ~90min ArcticL build), left it alone; 30min later daemon self-terminated mid-build ('.tldr/daemon.log: No client activity for 1800s, shutting down'); next 'tldr semantic' said 'daemon not started'. ROOT CAUSE: crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/daemon.rs:304-314 — idle check uses last_activity, ONLY updated on accepted client connections (line 323). Invisible to the idle loop: (1) the background warm/index build, (2) the in-process watcher's delta work. Any build longer than idle_timeout_secs is structurally unfinishable unattended. ADOPTED DESIGN (Codex-reviewed 2026-06-04; this issue = child A of epic TLDR-cxa, the presence-based liveness philosophy): ActivityTracker with PER-SOURCE timestamps (socket / cli-poke / watcher / internal) + busy tokens/refcount WITH AGE (RAII covers panic-unwind; hung spawn_blocking at daemon.rs:490/1183 must be visible as stale-busy, exposed via TLDR-qzc). Watcher tap: post-debounce/pre-filter (before watch_decision, watcher.rs:163) as AtomicU64 relaxed store, RE-EXCLUDING daemon self-writes (.tldr/store paths, watcher.rs:83) — counting own store writes = self-perpetuating immortality. Idle predicate: any(per-source presence fresh) || busy_refcount>0. Superseded options (a)/(b)/(c) and full history in comments. WORKAROUND until fixed: while true; do tldr daemon status >/dev/null 2>&1; sleep 600; done. Downstream: TLDR-qzc (status observability) -> TLDR-nke (CLI poke) -> TLDR-utj.7 (async warm ack); parallel: TLDR-axz (MCP parity), TLDR-yll (memory), TLDR-d26 (config migration).
close_reason: Fixed: presence-based ActivityTracker landed (commit on tldr-cli-daemon-support). Per-source timestamps + busy tokens with age; closure-owned guards on warm/delta spawn_blocking; watcher pre-filter tap excluding self-writes and Access events. 316 daemon tests green; 54 suite failures verified pre-existing (TLDR-6q2/TLDR-wks).

The fix must define activity as: socket accept OR build-in-progress OR watcher-delivered work — not just builds. Fix option (a) should consult both the build flag and a watcher last-delivery timestamp (or option (b)'s RAII guard held by both the build task and the delta worker).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: GOVERNING PHILOSOPHY for the fix (user, 2026-06-04): the idle question must change from 'is anyone talking to my socket?' to 'is anyone alive in this project?'. Daemon dies ONLY when the project is dormant. Liveness signals, ALL of which must reset/defer the idle timer: (1) ANY tldr CLI invocation — all ~64 surfaces, not just the ~18 daemon-routed ones; requires a cheap non-blocking fire-and-forget poke from non-routed commands when a daemon is registered for the project (must never slow the CLI down). (2) RAW watcher events, counted BEFORE the corpus filter — a long cargo build writing to target/ is filtered from indexing (correct) but is still proof of life (someone started that build); liveness taps the pre-filter event stream. (3) The daemon's own in-flight work — index build, delta processing ('never abandon your own job'). Idle shutdown fires only when no human, no agent, no file event, no internal work for idle_timeout. Accepted trade-off (explicit): on machines with long-running builds the daemon effectively never idles out — warm availability is chosen over memory thrift.

(2) WATCHER TAP: post-debounce/pre-filter (before watch_decision at watcher.rs:163, where the handler iterates at watcher.rs:161) as AtomicU64 relaxed store — but MUST re-exclude daemon self-writes (.tldr/store paths, currently excluded at watcher.rs:83): counting our own store writes as presence = self-perpetuating immortality. comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: IMPLEMENTED (2026-06-04, branch tldr-cli-daemon-support). New module crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/activity.rs: ActivityTracker with per-source AtomicU64 presence timestamps (Socket/CliPoke/Watcher/Internal; CliPoke reserved for nke) + busy tokens with label+age (Mutex<HashMap> for snapshot, lock-free AtomicUsize count for the 100ms idle loop). title: Daemon single-instance hardening: prevent 2 indexing daemons on the same folder
description:
Prevent >1 daemon indexing the SAME project folder (cross-project parallelism is intended & fine). Root cause of the load~147 cold-build storm: a busy (cold-building) daemon was misjudged dead, triggering restarts that slipped past the single-instance guard.

HOLE A (DONE 2026-06-03): start.rs resolved --project with a NON-canonical fallback when canonicalize() failed (current_dir().join). Under load that yields a DIFFERENT socket/PID-lock key for the SAME folder -> two daemons. FIX: resolve_project_root() now FAILS CLOSED ('cannot resolve project root') instead of falling back. Tests: existing-dir canonicalizes, missing-path errors, symlinked path resolves to same canonical root (same-folder => same-key). 6/6 lib start tests pass.

HOLE B (TODO): liveness must be judged by the OS, not the process. check_socket_alive is a bare socket connect; a daemon pegged at 100% CPU mid-~90min build can't answer -> misjudged dead -> start.rs reaps its socket -> restart races in. FIX: judge 'folder already owned' by PID flock held / kill(pid,0) on the registered PID (auto-released by kernel on death; no PID-reuse ambiguity with flock), NOT by a socket reply. Never reap a live owner's socket. Optionally expose a 'warming/building' state in the registry for UX (not for the liveness decision). Net guarantee: tldr daemon start on a folder whose daemon PID is alive can never spawn a second, even under restart spam.

Cross-ref: tldr-cli-demon supervisor re-issues 'daemon start' every tick + launchd KeepAlive — harmless once the start path is bulletproof. First-run thundering-herd across many projects (all launchd jobs cold-building at once) is a separate concern (stagger / global build semaphore).
close_reason: Both holes (canonicalize fail-closed + PID-liveness/locked-reap) implemented, Codex-reviewed, tested; same-folder duplicate-daemon prevention done. Deploy + optional flock-probe/cross-project-stagger noted as separate follow-ups.

Cross-repo — needs sequencing with ejm.

title: Filesystem-watch cutover: in-daemon notify watcher + C++ supervisor retirement
description:
EPIC (cross-repo program). Move filesystem watching OUT of the external C++ supervisor (tldr-cli-demon, which watches the tree and pokes the daemon via the IPC 'daemon notify' command) and INTO the Rust daemon via the notify crate + notify-debouncer-full: watcher -> internal mpsc channel -> reindex worker. Then reduce the C++ side to a launchd babysitter (ejm). Decision basis: bfc (Route 2).

WHY ITS OWN EPIC (split out of ac0, 2026-06-03): the watcher is not usearch-store work; it is a coupled, CROSS-REPO cutover. The C++ supervisor is NOT in this repo, so the bfc 'no dual watcher ownership' transition guard cannot be enforced from here alone. Shipping the Rust watcher ON while the C++ fsnotifier is still live = two writers racing deltas. So the cutover needs explicit sequencing across repos.

FOUNDATION ALREADY SHIPPED (in-repo): handle_notify already applies per-file deltas via IndexManager::apply_delta (t8f) + the is_corpus_file source filter (ac0.6); qr9 closed (invalidation off the async path). So ac0.2 = replace the coarse-invalidate + stubbed-reindex branch with a channel-fed worker that calls the SAME delta logic (refactor it into a shared process_dirty_file()).

DEFERRED DECISIONS (resolve inside this epic, not yet): (a) config-gate DEFAULT for the in-daemon watcher — default-OFF/opt-in vs ON vs env-gated (TLDR_IN_DAEMON_WATCH); (b) fate of the IPC Notify command / 'tldr daemon notify' CLI — keep as manual/git-hook poke vs remove.

DESIGN CONSTRAINTS for ac0.2 (advisor-validated 2026-06-03): watch the CANONICAL project root (FSEvents emits /private/var-style canonical paths; the store keys via root_relative — a non-canonical root makes every delta/delete silently MISS, the ss3 bug class). Add an EXPLICIT canonical store_dir+cache_dir exclusion in the event filter AND a startup precondition assert that they are outside the canonical root (the self-write-loop guard must be ENFORCED, not incidental via is_corpus_file's hidden-dir skip). Write the self-write trap test against an IN-TREE store so it is non-vacuous. Pin notify to the version notify-debouncer-full re-exports (avoid two notify versions). Hold the debouncer for the daemon lifetime (dropping it stops watching; drop-on-shutdown is the stop mechanism). Run one integration test through a SYMLINKED tmpdir so a path-canonicalization mismatch fails loudly.

- ac0.2 ships DEFAULT-ON (no config-gate).
- IPC Notify command + 'tldr daemon notify' CLI are KEPT as a manual/git-hook poke -> taf CLOSED.
- C++ side = DISABLE not delete (keep fsnotifier dormant; centralize the guard in respawn_fsnotifier across all 3 respawn sites; delete later). Tracked in tldr-cli-demon-pw5 (supersedes iyb).
- Warm-after-edit: disabling C++ fsnotifier ends supervisor-driven warm-after-edit; ACCEPTABLE because ac0.2 makes the Rust daemon own it in-process (startup/recovery warms unaffected).
CODEX-MANDATED ac0.2 scope (recorded on ac0.2): serialized + coalesced delta worker (older delta can overwrite newer; default-ON+unbounded ingress = burst storm), deletes bypass is_corpus_file, canonicalize path before salsa key, drop watcher before persist_stats. ac0.7 keeps the advanced (>200-file) burst-cap.

NET PATH = 2 moves: (1) tldr-code ac0.2 Rust watcher default-ON, (2) tldr-cli-demon disable guard. Children: ac0.2 + ejm open; c5d + taf closed.

title: Notify delta: source-files-only filter (§6 first gate) before chunk_file
description:
CORRECTNESS GAP found in the t8f P1 delta review (advisor). IndexManager::apply_delta runs chunk_file on ANY path a Notify carries. A non-corpus / ignored path (node_modules/, target/, generated files, the .tldr/ dir, unknown extensions, binaries) yields zero chunks -> apply_file_delta writes an EMPTY FileRecord for a file the full build never indexed, diverging the store's per-file records from what ProjectWalker would produce. A path that IS indexable-by-extension but excluded by ignore rules would get INDEXED by the delta though the build skips it. Either way the resident store drifts from a clean rebuild = the ss3 divergence class.

FIX: apply the SAME ProjectWalker ignore rules / source-file gate as the build, as the FIRST gate in apply_delta (design doc §6 'Source-files-only filter'), before chunk_file. Drop the Notify (no-op, store untouched) if the path is not an indexable corpus file. Edit AND delete paths must both honor it (a delete for an ignored path must not touch records).
acceptance_criteria: A Notify for a non-corpus/ignored path (node_modules, target, .tldr, unknown ext, binary) is dropped before chunk_file — no FileRecord written, store len unchanged; an indexable corpus-file Notify still deltas; gate uses the same rules as the build walker (no separate ignore list); covered by a unit/e2e test asserting an ignored-path Notify is a no-op.
notes:
DONE (2026-06-03). §6 first gate landed in IndexManager::apply_delta.
EDIT path: tldr_core::semantic::is_corpus_file(project, file) — single-file gate delegating to the SAME ProjectWalker/WalkBuilder stack as the build (gitignore + git_global + git_exclude + parents, DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_DIRS, generated-dir sentinels, JS/TS preserved dirs, is_binary_or_hidden, Language::from_path). Walks only the O(depth) ancestor chain of the target (filter_entry prunes non-ancestor dirs), so cost is bounded. No separate ignore list (AC requirement met).
DELETE path: store-as-source-of-truth (per advisor) — apply_file_delete is a verified clean no-op (Ok(0), no FileRecord written) for an unknown path, so removed==0 => Filtered (store untouched), removed>0 => Deleted{removed}. This inherits ALL build filter rules by construction (the store IS the walker's output) and zeroes divergence risk — REPLACED the earlier is_plausible_corpus_path path-replica (which could drift from the walker, e.g. JS/TS-preserved dist/).
ORDER (after advisor reorder): is_delete -> cheap cold-store model-capture short-circuit (Skipped, no walk) -> is_corpus_file edit filter (Filtered) -> enrich gate (NeedsRebuild) -> delete/edit. Cold churn (git checkout / npm install before first query) no longer pays a discarded walker build.
New DeltaOutcome::Filtered variant (distinct from cold-store Skipped). Caller (daemon.rs handle_notify) treats it as no-op (only NeedsRebuild invalidates).
TESTS: chunker_tests::is_corpus_file_* (5: accepts recognized source, rejects unknown ext / excluded dir / gitignored / missing file). index_manager tests (7 total): non-corpus edit paths (node_modules/target/unknown-ext/.git) -> Filtered store unchanged; ignored delete -> Filtered; gitignored .py -> Filtered; corpus-file delete -> Deleted{1} store_len 0->. seeded_manager now sets a FileRecord (prior delete test was vacuous without it). e2e test_semantic_delta_edit_keeps_count_and_delete_removes_all green. Full workspace builds; tldr-daemon/tldr-mcp tests green (one unrelated flaky perf microbench bench_cache_hit_latency 1.099us vs 1us target).

title: zxb.4: Wire daemon warm+query onto resident VectorStore
description: Replace Mutex<Option<SemanticIndex>> with Mutex<Option<VectorStore>> in DaemonState. Warm command: VectorStore::load (or build+save on miss) + corpus digest check. Query: embed query + search the resident store. Freshness check at load/notify time, NOT per query. Factor the search core from zxb.1 is reused here against the resident store. This is the biggest beneficiary — 4.3s JSON load on every warm becomes near-instant on the common (nothing changed) case.
close_reason: Done. Daemon semantic_index (Mutex<Option<SemanticIndex>>) replaced with semantic_store (Mutex<Option<(EmbeddingModel, VectorStore)>>). Warm uses load_or_build_store (near-instant on fresh store). Query uses query_store against resident store. Notify clears the slot. Build clean.

title: zxb.3: Wire embed command (embed.rs) onto VectorStore::build+save
description: Replace the manual chunk+EmbeddingCache+Embedder flow in embed.rs with VectorStore::build()+save(). The embed command becomes: build the store, persist it, report stats. EmbeddingCache stays as the build-time dedup layer inside VectorStore::build (not removed — that is TLDR-3gj).
close_reason: Done. embed.rs rewired to load_or_build_store(). Removed manual chunk/cache/embedder flow, --include-vectors flag (vectors in store). Build clean.

title: zxb.2: Wire CLI cold search path (semantic.rs) onto search_with_store
description: Replace SemanticIndex::build().search() at semantic.rs:153 with search_with_store(). Decide store_dir location (global cache sibling). Add store-outside-corpus guard. Dense-only path (non-hybrid). Hybrid --hybrid flag keeps SemanticIndex for now (see zxb.5).
close_reason: Done. CLI dense-only path wired to search_with_store(). store_dir_for() added to tldr-core for shared daemon/CLI path resolution. Hybrid stays on SemanticIndex (zxb.5). Build clean, no warnings.

title: zxb.1: Strip SemanticIndex fallback from store_search.rs (TLDR-lx7)
description: Foundation for all other zxb sub-tasks. Remove the SemanticIndex fallback branch from search_with_store(). On any VectorStore failure (load/build/search), propagate a detailed error message — never silently degrade. Changes: (1) Remove SemanticIndex import from store_search.rs. (2) Remove the Err(e) => { eprintln warn; SemanticIndex::build fallback } branch. (3) VectorStore errors propagate as TldrResult::Err with actionable messages. (4) Factor out the search core (embed query + search store + hits_to_report) so the daemon can reuse it against a resident store without the load/build wrapper. (5) Update tests. Does NOT touch any call site — just the helper module.
close_reason: Done. SemanticIndex fallback removed from store_search.rs. Three public functions: search_with_store (cold CLI), load_or_build_store (daemon warm), query_store (daemon per-query). All errors propagate. 7/7 unit tests pass, build clean.

title: DECISION: Route 2 — filesystem watching lives in the Rust daemon (notify crate); C++ supervisor reduced to launchd lifecycle
description: Chosen over keeping the external C++ supervisor (tldr-cli-demon) as the watcher driving the daemon via the IPC Notify command. The in-daemon notify-crate watcher (TLDR-ac0.2) is co-located with the in-RAM index it mutates: event -> internal mpsc channel -> reindex worker, no cross-process hop. Rationale: consolidation (one Rust process), dissolves TLDR-qr9 by construction, and makes incremental delta (t8f) an in-process channel not an IPC contract. Explicitly NOT a performance claim (notify and the C++ fsnotifier use the same OS primitives). The C++ supervisor shrinks to a launchd-backed babysitter or is retired (TLDR-ejm). HARD precondition: store/cache dir MUST be outside the watched corpus (self-write loop). User decision, 2026-06-02.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-02). Durable decision; closing so it stays queryable without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if revisited.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: REFINEMENT 2026-06-03: Route 2 stands. Cutover mechanics decided: the C++ fsnotifier is DISABLED (guard in respawn_fsnotifier, kept dormant) rather than deleted immediately; the Rust watcher ships DEFAULT-ON; IPC Notify is retained as a manual poke. 'No dual ownership' is satisfied by disabling C++, not by a staged config-gate (c5d closed). See epic TLDR-4vb + tldr-cli-demon-pw5.

title: DECISION: Daemon vector index is fully in-memory (RwLock-resident), not view()/mmap
description: The daemon holds the vector index FULLY IN RAM (load() into a writable Index behind the IndexManager RwLock, mutated in place), NOT served via usearch view()/mmap. Refines TLDR-zl8 (view-for-queries / load-for-mutation) into a two-path model: daemon/warm path = RAM-resident RwLock<VectorStore> with concurrent reads + in-place delta writes; cold CLI (no daemon) MAY view()/mmap or also load to RAM. CONSEQUENCE: the view()/mmap GC reader-grace race (TLDR-pdb) no longer governs daemon query+reindex safety (the RwLock does); pdb narrows to cold-CLI-only and may be moot. Compiler-proven prerequisite (memory concurrency-sync-evidence-2026-06-02): VectorStore + Arc<RwLock<VectorStore>> are Send+Sync with zero surgery. User decision, 2026-06-02.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-02). Durable decision; closing so it stays queryable without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if revisited.

title: In-daemon filesystem watcher (notify crate) — replace IPC Notify + C++ fsnotifier; rewrite handle_notify to channel
description: Bring file-watch detection INTO the Rust daemon via the notify crate (+ notify-debouncer-full), co-located with the in-RAM index it mutates. Watcher thread -> internal mpsc channel -> reindex worker. Removes the cross-process C++ fsnotifier->IPC hop and dissolves TLDR-qr9 by construction (invalidation flows via channel, never an async-thread mutex). The Notify IPC command becomes optional (keep as manual git-hook poke or delete). Rewrites the stubbed handle_notify (daemon.rs:1172 — currently nukes the whole index; comment admits 'In full implementation, would spawn background reindex task').
design: Route 2 (chosen 2026-06-02 over keeping C++ fsnotifier+IPC). Watch the canonical project root recursively on daemon start; stop on shutdown. Reuse chunker::enumerate_corpus_files as the SINGLE membership filter (watch-set == index-set, coupling-proof per kkt). HARD PRECONDITION: store_dir + cache_dir MUST be outside the watched corpus, else the daemon's own index writes fire events -> reindex -> write -> infinite loop (even more critical with a live watcher than with the poll gate). Debounce editor save-storms / git-checkout bursts via notify-debouncer-full. Honest framing: notify is NOT faster than fsnotifier (same OS primitives FSEvents/kqueue); the win is consolidation (one Rust process), killing qr9, and making t8f an in-process channel not an IPC contract — NOT performance.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon self-watches via notify; file create/modify/delete -> dirty events on internal channel (no IPC); a write INSIDE the store dir triggers nothing (self-write-loop trap test); qr9 invalidation no longer touches a lock on the async path.
notes:
2026-06-02 (Codex review): (1) CORRECT WORDING — handle_notify (daemon.rs:1172) is NOT a full stub: it already records dirty files, invalidates the salsa cache, and sets the resident index to None (coarse invalidation, daemon.rs:1188). ONLY the reindex branch is stubbed (daemon.rs:1202 'would spawn background reindex task'). Scope = replace coarse-invalidate + stubbed-reindex with channel-fed delta, not 'write the handler'. (2) TRANSITION GUARD — during cutover, ensure NO dual watcher ownership: the C++ fsnotifier must be disabled when the in-daemon notify watcher is active (don't run both → double events / races). Coordinate with TLDR-ejm.
2026-06-02 CORRECTION (de-stale): TLDR-qr9 is now CLOSED — its interim fix landed (handle_notify invalidation moved into an awaited spawn_blocking). So ac0.2 no longer 'dissolves an open bug'; it SUPERSEDES the interim spawn_blocking with the channel-fed design (watcher -> mpsc -> reindex worker). File:line refs here predate the qr9 commit and may be off by a few lines.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: DESIGN REFINEMENT (2026-06-02, post-zxb): The watcher feeds dirty events into the existing dirty_files set. Reindex is DEBOUNCED, not instantaneous — idle timeout OR threshold count triggers a batch reindex of only the accumulated dirty files. This prevents "compute always on" — the daemon is quiet while the user is actively editing, and processes changes in one batch when they pause. The zxb commit landed VectorStore in the daemon (Mutex<Option<(Model, VectorStore)>>), so the watcher now has a resident store to apply deltas to. The notify handler already clears the store on any change (coarse invalidation) — ac0.2 replaces this with fine-grained channel-fed delta via t8f.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
CODEX REVIEW (2026-06-03) — ac0.2 is UNDER-SCOPED as 'just add a watcher default-ON'. Must-fix before/with default-ON:
1) DELTA ORDERING (flaw): Notify IPC stays active + watcher worker both run deltas; apply_delta chunks/embeds OUTSIDE the write lock then applies under it (index_manager apply_file_delta accepts supplied vectors), so an OLDER delta can overwrite a NEWER one for the same file. FIX: serialize delta application — single worker draining the channel, deltas applied one-at-a-time; consider newest-wins/coalesce per file.
2) BURST STORM (flaw): default-ON + unbounded ingress risks a reindex storm on git checkout/npm install (each event chunks+embeds; the existing threshold is a stub that doesn't actually reindex/cap). FIX: bounded/coalesced channel + at least basic burst handling IN ac0.2 (this overlaps ac0.7 — fold the minimal coalescing in; leave advanced >200-file->full-rebuild cap to ac0.7).
3) DELETES must NOT be gated through is_corpus_file (returns false when the file is gone) — watcher filter forwards deletes straight to apply_delta's dedicated delete path; is_corpus_file is for edits/creates only.
4) SALSA KEY: handle_notify hashes the RAW path (salsa hash_path); watcher vs IPC canonical mismatch -> wrong key. Normalize/canonicalize the path before invalidate in the shared process_dirty_file().
5) Explicit store/cache exclusion is REQUIRED (is_corpus_file alone insufficient — daemon writes .tldr/cache + .tldr/daemon.log in-tree).
6) DROP the watcher before persist_stats on shutdown (persist writes .tldr/cache which the live watcher would see).
7) Hold debouncer in run() (not moved into a Send-spawned task); store-in-self surfaces Send/Sync on Arc<Self> spawns.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: DEP DECISION (2026-06-03): add ONLY 'notify-debouncer-full = "=0.7.0"' (exact-pin per repo convention; 0.8 is an RC) and access notify via its re-export (notify_debouncer_full::notify::*) — structurally avoids two notify versions (Codex). IMPL CAVEAT for checklist #4 (canonicalize path before salsa hash): must FIRST verify self.project is canonical (DAEMON.md claims identity uses canonicalized path) — if process_dirty_file canonicalizes the file but project is raw, root_relative/key_chunks key-mismatch (ss3 class) breaks the existing e2e delta tests (daemon.rs ~2165). Verify project canonicality + run the e2e delta tests as part of the refactor; don't canonicalize blindly.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: SCOPE CLARIFICATION 2026-06-03 (title is now partly stale): IPC Notify is KEPT (taf), not replaced — the watcher and the kept IPC Notify both feed the shared process_dirty_file(). The C++ fsnotifier is DISABLED (not replaced/removed) — see tldr-cli-demon-pw5. So the live title 'replace IPC Notify + C++ fsnotifier' reads as: KEEP IPC Notify, DISABLE C++ fsnotifier, add the in-daemon watcher (default-ON) as the primary source.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
STEP-1 CANONICALITY VERIFICATION (2026-06-03, pre-process_dirty_file). Verified all three key paths against source; result SIMPLIFIES the plan and DROPS the canonicalize-before-salsa-key fix.

1. self.project canonicality: PRODUCTION = canonical (start.rs:79 canonicalizes before TLDRDaemon::new at start.rs:178). TESTS = NON-canonical (daemon.rs:1312 passes temp.path(); macOS /var symlink). Construction-level canonicalization is OFF THE TABLE — it breaks assert_eq!(daemon.project(), temp.path()) at daemon.rs:1314 and the fake_path assert at 2321. This is BY DESIGN: self.project stays non-canonical and the downstream keying was hardened instead.

2. Vector-store delta keying ALREADY hardened (no per-file canonicalization needed): root_relative (vector_store.rs:997) does lexical strip -> canonical-strip-BOTH -> deterministic fallback; deleted_file_rel (index_manager.rs:398) strips against project AND project.canonicalize(), matching stored keys under a symlinked root (ss3 class) by construction. Canonicalizing the incoming file in apply_delta would BREAK deletes (a gone file cannot canonicalize) — this is the real reason for 'delete passthrough'. Net: pass the path through as-is to apply_delta.

3. Salsa query-cache key: BOTH sides hash the RAW client path. Registration handlers pass vec![hash_path(&file)] on the raw file from the command (daemon.rs:584/703/736/773/965); invalidation in handle_notify (daemon.rs:1093) hashes the raw Notify file the same way. They ALREADY match. Canonicalizing in notify ALONE would DIVERGE from the raw-path registration side -> strictly worse (invalidation would miss). DECISION: do NOT canonicalize before the salsa key. The 'canonicalize-before-salsa-key fix' is dropped; any symmetric canonicalization (both register+invalidate) is a separate, larger change out of ac0.2 scope.

CONSEQUENCE for process_dirty_file(): NO canonicalization anywhere. (a) hash raw file path for salsa invalidation (matches registration); (b) pass raw file path to apply_delta (robust keying + delete passthrough). Confirmed with advisor.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
IN-REPO IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE (2026-06-03), shipped DEFAULT-OFF pending cross-repo C++ disable. All gates green: cargo build clean; clippy 0 errors (no new warnings in changed files); watcher unit+smoke 7/7; full daemon suite 267 passed / 2 pre-existing ignored.

FILES CHANGED (tldr-code, branch tldr-cli-daemon-support):
- daemon.rs: extracted process_dirty_file() from handle_notify (canonicalization-free, delete-passthrough — see prior step-1 comment); handle_notify now delegates. Added ReindexOutcome struct. Wired spawn_watcher into run() under #[cfg(feature=semantic)] with a NAMED _watcher_guard (let _ would drop the Debouncer immediately). Added #[cfg(test)] dirty_file_count() observable.
- watcher.rs (NEW, semantic-gated): notify-debouncer-full(500ms) -> watch_decision() filter -> bounded mpsc(1024, try_send drop-on-full) -> single serialized coalescing worker (drains queue into a dedup HashSet, newest-wins; ordering intentionally discarded because apply_delta re-reads disk state) -> process_dirty_file(). spawn_watcher() returns Option<WatcherGuard>; refuses (logged) if store_dir is inside root. watch_decision() is a PURE fn (extracted per advisor) so trap/corpus/symlink/delete tests need no live daemon.
- types.rs: DaemonConfig.enable_watcher (#[serde(default)], DEFAULT FALSE). Default OFF because Move-2 (C++ fsnotifier disable) is CROSS-REPO (tldr-cli-demon / TLDR-ejm, still open); running both watchers double-feeds process_dirty_file (wasteful no-op via content_hash, but pointless). Flip to ON lands WITH the C++ disable.
- Cargo.toml (workspace + tldr-cli): notify-debouncer-full = =0.7.0; notify consumed via its re-export. Lockfile resolves single notify v8.2.0 — no two-version skew.

SELF-WRITE TRAP: closed by a PREFIX exclusion of in-tree <root>/.tldr (where persist_stats writes salsa_stats.json) + store_dir. The prefix check (not is_corpus_file) is load-bearing for DELETES — is_corpus_file canonicalizes and so returns false for a gone file. External store_dir (~/.cache/tldr/stores) is outside root by design; the trap test correctly targets in-tree .tldr, not the (vacuous) external store dir.

ACCEPTANCE STATUS: daemon self-watches via notify ✓; create/modify/delete -> dirty events on internal channel, no IPC ✓; in-store/.tldr write triggers nothing ✓ (trap test incl. delete); qr9 invalidation off the async lock path ✓ (channel->worker; semantic delta in spawn_blocking). REMAINING (not a code gap): flip enable_watcher default ON, coordinated with the cross-repo C++ fsnotifier disable (TLDR-ejm) to avoid dual ownership.

title: IndexManager: Arc<RwLock<VectorStore>> read/write split for concurrent queries + parallel reindex
description: Replace the daemon's coarse Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Option<SemanticIndex>>> with a thin IndexManager layer owning Arc<RwLock<VectorStore>>. Exposes exactly two methods — query(...) (read lock) and apply_delta(changes) (write lock) — so the daemon and watcher never touch a raw lock. Enables concurrent queries (daemon already spawns task-per-connection at daemon.rs:299; only the index Mutex serializes them today per the daemon.rs:544 comment) and sets up parallel reindex.
design: EVIDENCE (compiler-proven 2026-06-02, see memory concurrency-sync-evidence-2026-06-02): VectorStore + Arc<RwLock<VectorStore>> are Send+Sync with ZERO surgery (usearch Index = unsafe impl Send+Sync, all mutators &self; sidecar types Sync). The daemon.rs:122-123 '!Sync' comment is STALE — fix it as part of this work. PATTERN 'compute outside, mutate inside': embedding (slow, rayon-parallel) happens lock-free; only VectorStore::add/remove takes the brief write lock. CAVEATS: (1) read lock MUST be taken inside spawn_blocking, NEVER held across .await (else = qr9 shape); (2) write-lock window is NOT always sub-ms — VectorStore::add triggers reserve() (doubles + reallocates the whole index) on growth. CRATES: parking_lot (cleaner RwLock, no poisoning); arc-swap later only if lock-free reads are measured to matter.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon holds IndexManager<RwLock<VectorStore>> instead of Mutex<Option<SemanticIndex>>; concurrent queries verified (2+ in-flight take read locks simultaneously); stale !Sync comment removed; read lock only inside spawn_blocking.
notes:
2026-06-02 (Codex review): (1) ADD RESIDENT-LIFECYCLE ACCEPTANCE — today store_search creates a FRESH embedder per query (store_search.rs:202) and the daemon switches models via a single resident SemanticIndex slot (daemon.rs:557). IndexManager must specify: warm Embedder REUSE (no per-query construction), model-scoped store selection, load/rebuild/save timing, and daemon-shutdown behavior. (2) reserve() REALLOC: VectorStore::add grows capacity via reserve() when full (vector_store.rs:200) — a growth realloc under the write lock pauses ALL readers. Add pre-reserve (size to expected corpus + headroom) or batch-delta guidance so growth doesn't unexpectedly stall reads. (3) STALE !Sync COMMENTS to remove are in THREE places, not one: daemon.rs:122, index.rs:159, AND vector_store.rs:91 (claims usearch Index is not Sync).
2026-06-02: the 'remove 3 stale !Sync comments' sub-part is DONE (actually 6 comments; see closed chore TLDR-ac0.4) — that acceptance item is satisfied ahead of ac0.1 proper.
2026-06-02 CORRECTION (de-stale): the 'remove stale !Sync comments' sub-task is COMPLETE (landed in TLDR-ac0.4, committed @ tldr-cli-daemon-support). DISREGARD the earlier 'fix it as part of this work' / 'STALE comments to remove' lines in the design — that acceptance item is satisfied. REMAINING ac0.1 scope = the IndexManager/RwLock<VectorStore> itself (concurrent queries + parallel reindex). NOTE: file:line refs in this issue predate the ac0.4/qr9 commits and may be off by a few lines; locate by symbol, not line number.

title: Store freshness gate: detect source drift -> full rebuild (m01 reconcile-lite)
description: Codex review of m01 PR1 (commit d874b77): a loaded VectorStore is served as-is — VectorStore::load verifies persisted manifest/index integrity but does NOT check whether SOURCE files changed since the store was built. The manifest only invalidates on model/chunk-param/walker change, so search_with_store is currently build-once-until-config-changes: after a source edit it serves STALE vectors/rankings (and read_snippet reads current source lines against outdated line-range metadata). This is the design-doc §7.3 RECONCILE: on load, stat each persisted FileRecord {mtime,size,file_type} vs disk; on ANY drift (changed/added/removed/type-swapped file) do a FULL REBUILD. DISTINCT from TLDR-t8f, which is the incremental delta OPTIMIZATION (add/remove changed chunks without full re-embed); this is the coarse correctness gate t8f later optimizes. The store already persists FileRecords (vector_store.rs set_file_record/file_record) — the gate must enumerate the corpus the SAME way the chunker/walker does (same skip rules) or it rebuilds-always / misses deletions. HARD BLOCKER for wiring search_with_store into CLI/daemon/MCP (PR2).

Corpus-digest freshness gate implemented, tested (pure: determinism/add/edit/delete/0-chunk-trap; e2e real-model: rebuild-only-on-drift), and Codex-reviewed: 4 CORRECT, Item 3 TOCTOU BUG fixed (digest snapshot before chunk), Item 7 docs fixed. Items 5 (call-site store-location guard) + 6 (per-query walk -> load/notify time) deferred to zxb where wiring handles them. store_search now rebuilds on source drift instead of serving stale rankings. Unblocks zxb.

These must reflect REAL values (embedding tokenizer+weights revision, actual chunker parameters, walker/AST version) before the store ships, otherwise a model/recipe/walker change will NOT invalidate the on-disk store and load() will serve stale vectors as 'compatible'. The typed-LoadFail Incompatible path (b168c13) only protects config changes that are actually REFLECTED in ManifestId — placeholders defeat it.

title: Cache key strip_prefix silently falls back to raw path (can revert the absolute-vs-relative bug)
description: Codex review (2026-06-02, thread a708b3161b2e63d89). CacheKey::from_chunk (crates/tldr-core/src/semantic/cache.rs) strips the build root via strip_prefix and on FAILURE falls back to chunk.file_path.to_string_lossy() VERBATIM (the raw as-given path). For a symlinked root, a differently-normalized path, or a chunk outside the root, this silently re-creates the original absolute-vs-relative key-divergence (the TLDR-atc bug -> re-embed). walker.rs does NOT canonicalize (stores root.as_ref()) and the chunker stores path.to_path_buf(), so the 'all chunk paths are root-prefixed' assumption is unverified at the type level. FIX: canonicalize the root once + derive file_rel_path deterministically; on strip_prefix miss, log a WARNING and use a single canonical fallback (canonical absolute path), never the raw path. Same rule applies to the future usearch keys (l5d). Introduced with TLDR-atc cache-key fix (401a7ac).
close_reason: FIXED 2026-06-02 (commits 987af50 + 2d72df2). The silent strip_prefix raw-path fallback is hardened in BOTH locations: vector_store.rs root_relative (987af50) and cache.rs CacheKey::from_chunk via key_rel_path (2d72df2). Pattern: lexical strip -> canonical strip -> canonical-absolute (warned) -> raw (warned) — never a silent revert to the absolute-vs-relative divergence. Empty key_root (default/tests) still lexically matches -> full path unchanged. cache+vector_store tests pass.

title: Daemon handle_notify takes the semantic-index sync Mutex on the async thread (blocks runtime)
description: Codex review (2026-06-02, thread a708b3161b2e63d89). handle_notify (crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/daemon.rs) acquires the semantic_index Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Option<SemanticIndex>>> DIRECTLY in async code to invalidate it. The Semantic handler holds that same mutex across the whole build+search INSIDE spawn_blocking (correct). But a Notify arriving mid-build calls .lock() on the async thread and PARKS a Tokio worker for the entire build (seconds), defeating the spawn_blocking responsiveness fix (commit 7199f03). FIX: Notify must not lock the index on the async path — signal invalidation via an AtomicBool/mpsc channel (or do the lock inside spawn_blocking), keeping handle_notify O(1)/lock-free. See docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md section 8. Introduced with TLDR-atc spawn_blocking work.
notes:
2026-06-02: Do NOT fix in isolation. Route 2 chosen — TLDR-ac0.2 (in-daemon notify watcher) dissolves this by construction: invalidation moves to an internal mpsc channel, the async path never locks the index. Fix as part of ac0.2.
2026-06-02 (Codex review): RESOLVE AMBIGUITY. Two valid paths — (A) cheap interim, independently shippable NOW: move invalidation off the async path per this issue's original FIX (AtomicBool/mpsc; or take the lock inside spawn_blocking), keeping handle_notify O(1)/lock-free; (B) TLDR-ac0.2 supersedes by removing the IPC Notify path entirely. Prefer A if qr9 bites before ac0.2 lands (it is a live P1 runtime-blocking risk: handle_notify locks the sync mutex on the async path at daemon.rs:1188 while build/search holds it through blocking work at daemon.rs:550). If ac0.2 lands first, close qr9 as folded. NOT blocked by ac0.2 — A stays actionable.
close_reason: FIXED (interim, Codex before+after reviewed). handle_notify no longer locks the semantic_index std::sync::Mutex on the async path — moved to an awaited tokio::task::spawn_blocking (Arc::clone, poison-tolerant). Async worker no longer parked during an in-flight build. Ordering caveat + the ac0.2 superseding path documented inline. Build clean; 18 notify/daemon tests pass incl. test_semantic_index_invalidated_on_notify. Uncommitted in working tree (user controls commit/push). Full event-driven replacement remains TLDR-ac0.2.

title: Expand semantic eval gold set 14 -> 50-100 cases (single-case swings make all deltas untrustworthy)
description: The semantic_eval harness has 14 gold (query->file/fn) cases. At n=14, one case = 0.071 recall, so model/feature comparisons swing on a SINGLE query: e.g. ArcticL-vs-ArcticM looked like 'L is worse @5' but the entire difference was ONE query (parse imports rank 1->8) — i.e. within noise. This makes it impossible to trust the 4er-hybrid lift, the 9m2-descriptions lift, or any model choice. FIX: grow the gold set to 50-100 query->target pairs spanning the static layers (ast/cfg/dfg/callgraph), search, and semantic subsystems, with a mix of easy (lexically-obvious) and hard (modality-gap) queries, so recall@5/@10/MRR deltas become statistically meaningful. Gate trustworthy measurement of TLDR-4er and TLDR-9m2 on this.
acceptance_criteria: Gold set >=50 cases; a single-query rank change moves recall by <0.02; 4er/9m2 lifts measured against the expanded set with the deltas exceeding single-case noise.
close_reason: Gold-set expansion DONE. semantic_eval GOLD is now 52 cases (was 14) spanning ast/cfg/dfg/callgraph/search/security/quality/semantic subsystems, easy + modality-gap queries (semantic_eval.rs:28-294, comment credits TLDR-6h3). Acceptance met: >=50 cases (52); single-query swing = 1/52 = 0.019 < 0.02. Latest run (2026-06-02): R@5 0.750, R@10 0.827, MRR 0.599. The remaining acceptance clause ('measure 4er/9m2 lifts exceeding single-case noise') is a USE of this now-stable harness by those features when they land (TLDR-4er hybrid, TLDR-9m2 descriptions) — not remaining work on the gold set itself.

title: Incremental re-indexing: delta add/remove on file change instead of full re-embed
description:
Today every index build re-chunks the whole tree and re-embeds anything whose content-hash changed; a full rebuild is slow. usearch supports remove(key)/add(key) on a writable RAM index, enabling INCREMENTAL updates: on a file change, re-chunk ONLY that file, remove its stale function-keys, add the changed ones — a few-ms-per-file delta instead of a full rebuild. REQUIRES a RAM-resident writable index (view() is read-only #97) -> lives in the daemon (TLDR-atc, DONE) on the usearch store (TLDR-l5d).

FULL DESIGN: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md.

Key scheme (RECONCILED with the doc — supersedes the earlier 'path+name+signature hash' wording): stable u64 = hash("{file_rel_path}::{class_name}::{function_name}::{ordinal}"), root-relative; ordinal disambiguates duplicate names within a file; file-level chunks use "{file_rel_path}#file". (Ordinal is positional -> same-name insert churns the key, but bounded by the content-addressed dedup layer = cache hit, no real re-embed.)

Delta classifier: EMBED (body content_hash changed) / META-ONLY (unchanged body, line shift) / REMOVE (deleted). Debounce: 750ms quiet + 5s max-wait + source-only filter + burst cap (200 files / 1000 events per 2s) -> full rebuild. handle_notify MUST stay lock-free on the async thread (see TLDR-qr9). Reconcile on restart = per-file mtime+size vs stored record (no all-files hashing).

DEPENDS ON l5d shipping the sidecar + manifest + per-file records: the delta needs per-file key lookup + content_hash tracking that can't re-chunk the whole tree, which is exactly why those persist in l5d (P0), not t8f.
acceptance_criteria: Editing one file re-embeds only that file's changed functions (not the corpus); deleted functions' vectors are removed; query results reflect the change within one daemon save cycle; full rebuild still available as fallback.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: Design doc written: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (committed 73cf41f). Covers stable u64 chunk keys + metadata sidecar + per-file key index, the embed/meta-only/remove delta classifier, debounce/coalesce pipeline w/ burst cap, atomic save + startup reconcile, spawn_blocking+Mutex concurrency, edge cases, full-rebuild fallback, 4-phase rollout (P0 usearch store=l5d -> P1 delta core -> P2 debounce -> P3 persistence). Gated on l5d (usearch writable RAM index; view() read-only NOT used at our scale).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: DESIGN REFINEMENT (2026-06-02, post-zxb): Debounced batch reindex is the confirmed flow. NOT instantaneous per-save. Flow: file save -> add to dirty set -> threshold hit OR idle timeout -> batch reindex only the dirty files. This keeps compute bursty (process accumulated changes when user pauses) not constant (no re-embed on every keystroke). The ONNX model stays loaded in the daemon so there is zero per-batch startup cost. A few changed chunks embed in milliseconds. The dirty_files HashSet + auto_reindex_threshold already exist in daemon.rs — t8f builds the delta logic on top of that accumulation. Key parameters: debounce quiet=750ms, max-wait=5s (from design doc), burst cap=200 files (triggers full rebuild instead of delta).

TLDR_ENRICH). Deltas are RAM-only (daemon never persists the store; restart reloads pre-delta disk store, freshness gate rebuilds).
