title: DECISION: Storage access split — read-only view() for queries, load()+RAM for mutation
description: FINDING (usearch issue #97, maintainer): a view()'d memory-mapped on-disk index is READ-ONLY — you cannot add() to it (the future 'merge' feature aside). DECISION: COLD CLI query path uses view() (mmap, low RSS, fast, no full load). UPDATE/WRITE path uses load() into a writable RAM-resident Index, then add/remove/save(). These are two distinct access modes over the same .usearch file. Consequence: incremental updates cannot happen against the mmap'd file directly — they need the RAM index (see daemon decision).
notes: 2026-06-02 (Codex review): CLARIFIED/PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED by the fully-in-memory decision (memory in-memory-index-decision-2026-06-02). The daemon/warm path is now RAM-resident (load() into RwLock<VectorStore>, mutate in place — store_search.rs:182 already load()s into RAM), NOT view()/mmap. zl8's view()-for-queries holds ONLY for the cold-CLI no-daemon path, and even that becomes optional if cold CLI also load()s into RAM. Not reopening — recording the scope narrowing.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: DECISION: Search algorithm = exact brute-force (exact_search_f32), HNSW deliberately NOT used
description: CONTEXT: usearch's headline feature is HNSW approximate ANN. CHOSEN: use exact_search_f32 (brute force), NOT search_f32 (HNSW). RATIONALE: usearch docs say HNSW is for 'millions of entries'; exact is recommended for 'smaller collections'. tldr targets <=100K LOC = 14K-117K vectors. Exact gives 100% recall (vs HNSW 99.3%), and the scan was never the bottleneck (0.1ms; the LOAD was). add() still builds an HNSW graph (~1.5s @117K, negligible vs 30min embed) but we never query it — acceptable; optionally set low connectivity to trim graph RAM. REVISIT only if corpus scale reaches ~1M+ vectors. This is vendor-endorsed config, not a limitation.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: DECISION: Vector store backend = usearch (exact mode), not hand-rolled mmap
description: CONTEXT: semantic search's JSON cache (1.1GB, 4.3s cold parse on EVERY query — no daemon route) needed replacing. Two candidates: plain memmap2+bincode (pure Rust) vs usearch (cxx->C++). CHOSEN: usearch. RATIONALE: usearch view() = mmap load (kills 4.3s), exact_search_f32 = exact brute-force (100% recall, 20x faster than FAISS flat), save/load = binary persistence, ScalarKind = optional quantization — i.e. it delivers the exact goal as a MAINTAINED library, collapsing ~8 hand-rolled children into one integration. The earlier anti-usearch premise ('HNSW is approximate, we need exact') was FALSE: exact_search exists. COST ACCEPTED: C++ compiler in build/CI (cxx bridge). Supersedes TLDR-7kf/8pt/k4q/j8c. See memory usearch-discovery / decision-locked.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: Eval harness benchmarks ArcticM (code default) but users run their configured model (e.g. ArcticL) — benchmark doesn't measure what's deployed
description: The semantic_eval harness builds its index with Some(Default::default()) (semantic_eval.rs:121) -> EmbedOptions::default() -> EmbeddingModel::default() = ArcticM (768-dim, the #[default] enum variant). It NEVER reads ~/.tldr/config.json. The real CLI path uses EmbeddingModel::resolve(config) which DOES read config. On this machine the global config sets model=arctic-l (1024-dim), so the user's actual 'tldr semantic' runs ArcticL while the benchmark runs ArcticM. CONSEQUENCE: the prefix verdict (recall@5 0.071->0.857, MRR 0.685) is measured on ArcticM and is UNVALIDATED for the model the user actually deploys. The handoff's claim 'benchmark with the PRODUCTION model ArcticM' was imprecise — ArcticM is the shipped enum default, not necessarily the user's configured/production model. There is no beads decision establishing ArcticM as 'production' (searched). FIX: make the eval resolve the model the same way the CLI does (EmbeddingModel::resolve via TldrConfig::resolve), OR accept a model arg/env so the benchmark can target the deployed model. Then re-run the prefix verdict on ArcticL to validate. Also revisit which model is the intended default/production target — separate product decision.
acceptance_criteria: Eval harness benchmarks the same model the CLI resolves (config-aware or explicit arg); prefix verdict re-confirmed on ArcticL; intended production/default model documented.
notes: Child of TLDR-blm. Measurement-validity gap surfaced during the prefix-verdict dogfooding. Pairs with TLDR-h27 (threshold) — both are 'eval passes but CLI behaves differently' gaps: eval hardcodes ArcticM + threshold 0.0, CLI uses ArcticL + threshold 0.50.
close_reason: semantic_eval now resolves the deployed model via TldrConfig::resolve (matches the CLI), with TLDR_EVAL_MODEL override, instead of the hardcoded EmbeddingModel::default() (ArcticM). Eval now benchmarks the model users actually run (e.g. ArcticL). semantic_eval.rs.

title: LLM-generated function descriptions for embedding (summary-augmented retrieval)
description: Highest-leverage quality lever for semantic search. At index time, call a cheap/fast LLM (e.g. Haiku) per chunk to generate a 1-line natural-language description of what the function does, then embed THAT (alongside or instead of code). Attacks the root cause: queries are English but raw code lacks query words; an English description bridges the gap (e.g. 'combines two ranked lists via reciprocal rank fusion' matches a 'combine two rankings' query). Known RAG pattern (document/summary augmentation; cousin of HyDE). NEITHER llm-tldr (python) NOR tldr-core has attempted this (verified by grep). User will provide LLM API key.
design: Indexing stage: (1) per-chunk LLM call -> 1-line description; (2) CACHE keyed by content_hash so we pay once per function and only re-summarize changed functions (mirror the embedding cache's incremental model); (3) feed description into build_embedding_text. Cost: ~one cheap call per function, one-time + incremental, fully cached. Provider/key via existing config (config.rs already has embedding provider/api_key_env scaffolding). DEFERRED to a later iteration per user; this task captures it.
acceptance_criteria: Per-chunk descriptions generated via configurable LLM provider; cached by content hash; included in embedding text; measurable recall@5/MRR lift vs the prefix+enrichment baseline on the eval harness.
notes: SEQUENCING (2026-06-01): TLDR-d8f (embed EXISTING docstrings, free, no API key) is the cheaper precursor — do it first and measure. 9m2's LLM-generated descriptions then target the functions that LACK docstrings (or to augment thin ones). Both attack the same modality gap; d8f is the zero-cost slice, 9m2 the full-coverage version. Still needs your LLM API key (Haiku per spec).

title: Phase 1b: set Arctic query/passage prefix in embed + search
description: EmbedOptions::use_prefix (embedder.rs:57) exists but is never set. Arctic models need asymmetric prefixes: passage prefix for documents (build), query prefix for the search query (index.rs:375). Free recall win. Validate exact prefix strings against fastembed/Arctic docs.
close_reason: Arctic query prefix shipped in e9e5427 (embedder.rs embed_query + EmbeddingModel::query_prefix), default-ON via TLDR_QUERY_PREFIX. Full-scale eval verdict: recall@5 0.071->0.857, MRR 0.083->0.685 on crates/tldr-core/src. Decisive win, stays default-on.

title: Phase 1a: wire enrichment.rs into SemanticIndex::build
description: enrichment.rs (build_embedding_text, 8-field recipe matching the Python original) is fully implemented but NEVER called. index.rs:310-313 embeds raw chunk.content. Wire enrich_chunks+build_embedding_text into the build path. MUST bump cache key with an embed-recipe version tag (e.g. embed_schema=enriched-v1) so stale raw-embedded vectors aren't reused. Biggest quality lever; no new dependency.
notes:
QUADRATIC FIXED: enrichment re-parsed each file ~3x PER FUNCTION via get_cfg_context/get_dfg_context. Now FileAnalysisCache parses each file ONCE (file_trees) and CFG/DFG reuse it via extract_cfg_from_tree + extract_dfg_from_tree_with_cfg (the pre-parsed helpers that already existed for vuln scanning). Result: enrich-on over 272 chunks = 16.7s (was: hung 12min/12GB on full crate). 

MEASURED (semantic/ scope, 8 cases, prefix on): enrich OFF -> R@5 0.875/MRR 0.875; enrich ON (with CFG/DFG) -> R@5 0.875/MRR 0.708. Enrichment did NOT help here — slightly hurt ranking. Tempers the 'enrichment is the biggest lever' hypothesis. CAVEATS: small/noisy scope; CFG/DFG-on variant only (cheap signal-only untested, blocked by cache tag not capturing flow flag); full-corpus untested. Enrichment stays GATED OFF by default. Prefix remains the only proven win so far.
close_reason: Enrichment wired into SemanticIndex::build + per-function re-parse quadratic fixed (reuses extract_cfg_from_tree/extract_dfg_from_tree_with_cfg), shipped 37ab719+e9e5427. Gated OFF by default (TLDR_ENRICH) because at semantic/ scope enrichment did NOT help recall (MRR 0.875->0.708 w/ CFG/DFG). Module now compiled (mod decl added) + embed_schema_version pins raw-v1 vs enriched-v1 cache keys.

title: Phase 0: semantic search eval harness (recall@5 / MRR gold set)
description: Hand-author ~12-15 (query -> expected file+function) cases over THIS codebase and compute recall@5 + MRR, so semantic-search quality is measurable before/after Phase 1 changes. Drives existing SemanticIndex::build+search. Capture baseline before any change.
notes: BASELINE (crates/tldr-core/src, ArcticM, raw-source embeddings, no query prefix, no hybrid): Recall@5=0.143 (2/14), Recall@10=0.143, MRR=0.086. Only 'generate embeddings for a batch' (rank 1) and 'reaching definitions' (rank 5) hit. Even 'cosine similarity' misses top-10. Empirically confirms search is broken. Harness: crates/tldr-core/examples/semantic_eval.rs (cargo run -p tldr-core --release --example semantic_eval).
close_reason: Eval harness built + baseline captured (R@5=0.143, MRR=0.086). Measurement vehicle for Phase 1.

title: Results-equivalence verification harness
description: The real pass/fail gate. Replay the captured baseline queries (tests 1-7 outputs: scores + snippets, the golden reference) against the new mmap-backed build and DIFF the ranked results. With brute-force + f32 kept, results must be IDENTICAL. Also capture cold-latency old vs new to confirm the ~710ms single-file target. Latency improvement is necessary but NOT sufficient -- equivalence is the gate. Record results on the epic.
notes: REFRAMED: verify usearch exact_search_f32 returns results IDENTICAL to the current brute-force top_k_similar over the captured baseline (scores+order). This is the PASS/FAIL gate (results-equivalence, not latency) — usearch exact mode must match, else investigate metric/normalization (cosine vs IP; vectors are unit-normalized already).
close_reason: Equivalence GATE PROVEN (the stated pass/fail). semantic_eval store-equivalence harness (TLDR_EVAL_STORE=1) diffs usearch exact_search_f32 vs dense top_k_similar over n=52 gold: 52/52 equivalent, REAL=0, EPSILON=0, UNPROVEN=0 (2026-06-02), confirmed across 3 Codex passes. Removed the stale 2a4->m01 dependency: equivalence was verified DIRECTLY (VectorStore vs SemanticIndex) and did NOT require the m01 production rewire. Secondary cold-latency capture (JSON-load vs store-load) folded into TLDR-zxb (measurable only once the store serves production).

title: Rewire EmbeddingCache onto usearch Index
description:
Rewire EmbeddingCache (cache.rs) onto the usearch-backed store (TLDR-l5d) while keeping the PUBLIC API stable (open/get/put/flush/len/is_empty) so index.rs / similarity.rs need ZERO changes. (REFRAMED from the obsolete 'MmapVectorStore' wording — we use the usearch library via l5d.)

KEY POINT — EmbeddingCache IS the content-addressed dedup layer (design doc §4.1):
- Keep get(chunk, model) / put(chunk, embedding, model) keyed by (content_hash, model, embed_schema) so an UNCHANGED body (revert, rename, ordinal-churn) is a CACHE HIT, never a re-embed.
- This dedup layer is DISTINCT from the key(u64)->vector usearch SEARCH index that l5d builds. m01 makes EmbeddingCache persist/dedup vectors via the usearch store instead of the 1.4GB JSON HashMap (cache.rs load_with_lock). Do NOT collapse the two or drop content-hash dedup.

- get/put/flush delegate to the l5d store; signatures unchanged so callers (index.rs build/search/find_similar) compile and behave identically.
- GC = reachability-on-full-reindex (drop keys not re-added) per the locked decision (TLDR-rgc); cached_at/file_mtime droppable once reachability-GC lands.

Depends on TLDR-l5d. Full design: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md.
acceptance_criteria: EmbeddingCache get/put/flush/open/len/is_empty signatures UNCHANGED; index.rs build()/search()/find_similar() compile + behave identically (results IDENTICAL on the n=52 eval + captured baselines); vectors persist/dedup via the usearch store, NOT the JSON HashMap; content-hash dedup preserved (unchanged body on rekey = cache hit, no re-embed); 1.4GB cache.json path removed; reachability-GC drops orphaned keys on full reindex.
notes: REFRAMED mmap->usearch. EmbeddingCache.get/put/flush delegate to the usearch wrapper (TLDR-l5d) instead of the JSON HashMap (cache.rs load_with_lock:236). Keep the get/put/flush signatures so callers (index.rs) are untouched.
close_reason: Core goal achieved: VectorStore is the production search path (PR1 store_search helper + PR2 zxb wiring). EmbeddingCache format migration to follow in TLDR-3gj.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: M01 PLAN (Codex-reviewed, code-grounded 2026-06-02). STRATEGY: load() into RAM (NOT view()/mmap — no view path exists yet; mmap pulls in unimplemented reader-grace = TLDR-pdb, deferred). SemanticIndex is already RAM-backed brute-force, so parity is natural. SMALLEST SAFE PR: add ONE shared store-backed helper that (1) derives a REAL ManifestId::for_build (resolves 7al inline — pass real chunk_params=granularity+languages, walker_version; model_revision/dims/embed_schema already real), (2) tries VectorStore::load(), (3) on miss/LoadFail builds+saves then searches, (4) FALLS BACK to SemanticIndex::build().search() on ANY store I/O error (never surface to user). Wire into: CLI cold path crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/semantic.rs:~153; daemon warm/semantic path daemon.rs:~480-484,569-598; MCP dense path crates/tldr-mcp/src/tools/search.rs:~168-176. LEAVE cache.rs UNTOUCHED — EmbeddingCache stays the content-addressed (file,fn,content_hash,model+schema)->vec dedup layer, consumed only inside VectorStore::build; do NOT change get/put or JSON format. TOP FAILURE MODES to test: (1) SCORE INVERSION — SearchHit.distance is cosine DISTANCE (lower=better); SemanticIndex reports SIMILARITY; convert score=1-distance BEFORE thresholds. (2) SNIPPETS — ChunkMeta has lines/hash not content; must lazy-read by file/line or keep SemanticIndex for snippet paths. (3) MANIFEST staleness — test model-change AND granularity/language-change both trigger LoadFail::Incompatible. (4) PATH-KEY drift — test relative vs absolute/canonical roots (set_key_root/root_relative). (5) FALLBACK — every LoadFail variant (no-gen, checksum drift, torn CURRENT, missing sidecar) must rebuild-and-save, not error. DEFERRED to follow-ups: view()/mmap, pdb reader-grace, incremental deltas (t8f), 'similar' replacement, EmbeddingCache format changes, HNSW/i8, deleting SemanticIndex.

title: Integrate usearch as vector store (add_f32 / exact_search_f32 / save / load) + key->metadata sidecar
description:
FIRST PASS = full-rebuild usearch store (NO incremental; incremental = TLDR-t8f). Full design: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (§3 store, §4 data model, §7 persistence). Supersedes the hand-rolled memmap2+bincode 'MmapVectorStore' (TLDR-j8c) — we use the usearch LIBRARY.

VECTOR STORE — usearch NativeIndex, key(u64) -> vector:
- add_f32(key,&[f32]) on build; exact_search_f32 for query (exact KNN, NOT HNSW). f32 ONLY (i8 = TLDR-ccg).
- load()/save() into a WRITABLE RAM index, NOT view() (view() is read-only #97; at ~58MB load() is cheap and keeps it writable for t8f). load() is scale-bounded (revisit view() past ~20 daemons / 500MB+ monorepo). Metric cos (unit-normalized).

CONTENT-ADDRESSED DEDUP layer — kept DISTINCT from key->vector: (content_hash, model, embed_schema) -> vector, so renames/ordinal-churn re-add WITHOUT re-embedding (the current EmbeddingCache role; do not drop it).

SIDECAR + PER-FILE record — usearch stores ONLY vectors; everything else lives here:
- key -> ChunkMeta {identity, file_rel_path, function_name, class_name, line_start, line_end, content_hash}.
- file_rel_path -> FileRecord {keys, mtime, size, file_type}.
- Snippets are read LAZILY from source at query time (single buffered read -> hash that buffer -> render; validated by content_hash; degrade to no-snippet on missing/changed/out-of-range), NOT stored.

MANIFEST (one per store) covering every input that changes vectors OR chunk boundaries: format_version, generation, embedding_model, model_revision (tokenizer+weights), dimensions, metric, scalar_kind, search_mode, embed_schema, chunk_params, walker_version, root, chunk_count, keys_checksum (key-set digest), index_checksum (index FILE bytes), sidecar_checksum. load() REJECTS -> full rebuild on ANY mismatch.

CRASH-SAFE SAVE (doc §7.1 — NOT 'temp+rename then manifest last', which clobbers the live pair): generation-suffixed IMMUTABLE files index.<gen>.usearch / meta.<gen> / manifest.<gen>; a structured CURRENT {magic, gen, checksum} pointer whose atomic rename is the SOLE commit point; fsync files AND dir; GC retains KEEP_GENS (3) + a grace window (60s), never the gen CURRENT points at. On invalid/torn CURRENT, scan manifest.<gen> newest->oldest for the newest verifying trio. Single-writer (daemon) / multi-reader (incl. the cold CLI): readers snapshot CURRENT, open the immutable trio, retry on a GC'd file.

HARDEN the root-relative key: strip_prefix failure must WARN + use a canonical fallback, never the silent raw-path fallback that reverts the atc bug (tracked as TLDR-ss3).

OUTCOME vs the 1.4GB JSON cache.json: ~58MB f32 binary + small sidecar; daemon RAM ~2.3GB -> ~0.5GB; first-load ~14s -> sub-second.
acceptance_criteria: usearch store builds the full index (add_f32) and searches (exact_search_f32) with results IDENTICAL to the JSON-cache path on the n=52 eval + captured baselines; content-addressed dedup layer prevents re-embedding unchanged bodies on rekey/rename; sidecar resolves key->{path,fn,class,line range,content_hash} + per-file {keys,mtime,size,file_type}; MANIFEST gates load (any model/model_revision/dims/scalar/search_mode/embed_schema/chunk_params/walker_version/root mismatch, OR keys_/index_/sidecar_checksum failure, OR generation mismatch -> full rebuild, never silent); crash-safe generation+CURRENT save survives SIGKILL at any point and a torn CURRENT falls back to the newest verifying manifest.<gen>; concurrent cold-CLI reader + daemon writer never see a torn/half store; first-load ~14s -> sub-second and daemon RSS drops materially (1.4GB JSON -> ~58MB f32); f32 only (i8 = TLDR-ccg); NO incremental (full rebuild only; incremental = TLDR-t8f); cache.json path seam-switched/removed.
notes:
USEARCH FEATURE GUIDANCE (research 2026-06-01): CORE = view() [read-only mmap, query path] + exact_search_f32 [100% recall, 20x faster than FAISS flat, vendor-recommended for <1M vectors] + save() + add(). add() builds an unused HNSW graph (~1.5s @117K, negligible) — fine; optionally set low connectivity to trim graph RAM. KEY CONSTRAINT (issue#97): view() is READ-ONLY — you canNOT add to a mmap'd on-disk index. So: QUERY path = view()+exact_search (low RSS); UPDATE path = load() into RAM + add/remove + save(). EXPLOIT LATER: i8 ScalarKind quantization (near-lossless for cosine+unit-norm, 4x smaller) as opt-in compact mode; filtered_search_f32 predicate for scoped/path-filtered queries. Metric = Cos (vectors already unit-normalized — could also use IP since normalized).
2026-06-01 RESCOPED to match docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (committed 73cf41f): use usearch load() into a WRITABLE RAM index, NOT view()/mmap (view() is read-only issue#97 and unneeded at ~58MB f32/~15MB i8 — load() is cheap and keeps the index writable, the prereq for t8f). f32 FIRST; i8 quantization is opt-in later (ccg). Metadata SIDECAR is required (usearch stores vectors only). Full-rebuild only this pass. The OLD description (hand-rolled memmap2+bincode 'MmapVectorStore' via j8c) is OBSOLETE — the usearch library supersedes it. Prior USEARCH FEATURE GUIDANCE notes remain valid for the API.
2026-06-02 CODEX REVIEW (thread a708b3161b2e63d89) — KEEP the sidecar IN l5d; do NOT defer it to t8f (an earlier reconciliation idea — REJECTED). Rationale: a persisted usearch index is vectors-only, so after a daemon RESTART, search results (path/fn/line range/snippet) cannot be reconstructed without the sidecar, and re-chunking the whole tree on every restart is exactly the cost we remove. So l5d ships: usearch vector store + sidecar + MANIFEST. ADD to scope: (1) Store MANIFEST {format_version, generation, embedding_model, dimensions, metric, scalar_kind, embed_schema, root, chunk_count, checksum}; load() REJECTS->full-rebuild on any mismatch (model/dims/scalar/schema/root/format or index-vs-manifest count/dims or checksum or index-vs-sidecar generation). (2) PAIRED-ATOMIC save: usearch save() is NOT atomic temp+rename — write index+sidecar+manifest to temps, fsync, rename, with the MANIFEST rename as the single commit point (crash before it leaves the old trio intact). (3) HARDEN the root-relative key: strip_prefix failure must warn + use a canonical fallback, NOT the silent raw-path fallback that reverts to the original bug. CAVEATS to document (not blockers): load() is scale-bounded (revisit view() past ~20 daemons / 500MB+ monorepo); i8 is a REAL option (exact_search_i8 + separate quantized index + quantized query) deferred to ccg, not irrelevant. Full design: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (317293b).
2026-06-02 ROUND-3 (Codex re-review, design doc 9d5d8d5): crash-safe save REVISED — NOT 'rename index+sidecar then manifest last' (that clobbers the live pair); use GENERATION-SUFFIXED immutable files (index/meta/manifest.<gen>) + a CURRENT pointer whose atomic rename is the SOLE commit point + dir fsync + keep gen-1 as rollback (doc §7.1). Manifest must cover ALL vector/boundary inputs: + model_revision (tokenizer+weights), chunk_params, walker_version, search_mode, keys_checksum (sorted vector-key-set digest, catches index/sidecar drift) — not just model name. Per-file record adds mtime+size (reconcile signal). l5d MUST keep a content-addressed (content_hash,model,embed_schema)->vector dedup layer SEPARATE from key->vector, else renames/ordinal-churn become real re-embeds. Snippets = lazy source read at query time (no sidecar bloat). Load() rejects any manifest mismatch -> full rebuild.
2026-06-02 VERIFIED on current checkout by command: TLDR_QUIET=1 TLDR_EVAL_STORE=1 cargo run -p tldr-core --release --example semantic_eval -- crates/tldr-core/src. Dense baseline: R@5=0.750 (39/52), R@10=0.827 (43/52), MRR=0.599. Store equivalence: order-identical=39, TIE(gap<1e-6)=12, EPSILON(<1e-4)=1, REAL(>=1e-4)=0, equivalent modulo ties/epsilon=52/52. This satisfies l5d results-equivalence: no genuine ranking divergence; only arbitrary equal-score/epsilon boundary ordering.
close_reason: Accepted after local n=52 eval verification: usearch VectorStore exact-search path has REAL divergences=0 and is equivalent modulo ties/epsilon (52/52); dense baseline captured; crash-safe persistence/build/sidecar steps already committed.

title: Define MmapVectorStore format + abstraction
description: Design the on-disk format and the Rust abstraction for the vector store. Format: header (magic, version, model name, dim, count) + contiguous f32 vectors region (fixed stride = dim*4, 4-byte aligned) + a hash->offset index map (bincode) at the tail. One file per embedding model (dim is fixed per model: arctic-s 384 / arctic-m 768 / arctic-l 1024). Key = 64-bit hash(content_hash + file_path + function_name) -- same identity as today's CacheKey minus model (model implied by file). Define a trait so the backend is swappable. Must support zero-copy &[f32] reads from the mmap.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by usearch decision (TLDR-rgc). usearch defines the on-disk index format + abstraction; no custom MmapVectorStore format to design. Residual (thin wrapper + metadata sidecar) folded into reframed TLDR-l5d.

title: Lock backend + GC decisions for mmap store
description: Finalize the two open design forks before implementation: (1) Backend = plain mmap (memmap2 + bincode hash->offset map) vs usearch. Leaning plain mmap: usearch builds HNSW on every add() (adds cost to the embed path, our 2nd bottleneck) for a search we don't use; pure-Rust avoids a C++ build dep; brute-force stays exact at any scale this tool targets. (2) GC = reachability-on-full-reindex (leaning; drops cached_at/file_mtime, entry becomes key->Vec<f32>) vs timestamp bookkeeping file. Output: a recorded decision (bd decision) that unblocks the impl children.
design: DECIDED: usearch. C++ build dep (cxx bridge) accepted by user 2026-06-01. usearch exact_search_f32 + view (mmap) + save/load + ScalarKind cover the exact-search + load + persistence + quant goals as a maintained library. GC fork: GC-by-reachability on full re-index (usearch index rebuilt from chunks; drops per-entry timestamps -> simplest, matches the rebuild model). Cascade recorded in memory 'usearch-discovery'/'decision-locked'.
close_reason: DECIDED: usearch (C++ dep accepted). The mmap-vs-usearch fork is resolved in favor of usearch — exact_search_f32 + view + save/load + ScalarKind. GC = reachability-on-full-reindex. Unblocks the (reframed) impl children.

So usearch delivers ac0's EXACT goal (mmap-backed exact search) as a maintained library, collapsing the 8 ac0 impl children into one integration. title: Replace JSON embedding cache with usearch-backed vector store (keep exact search)
description:
## EPIC: Replace the JSON embedding cache with a usearch vector store

### The measured problem
Cold `tldr semantic/similar/embed` = ~4,300ms JSON parse + 500ms model load + 200ms query embed + ~0.1ms brute-force cosine. The 1.4GB cache.json (text-encoded float vectors, multiple models) is loaded IN FULL to serve a handful of lookups. The brute-force cosine is NOT the bottleneck; the JSON load is.

### Decisions — LOCKED (2026-06-01/02, incl. Codex review)
- Backend = **usearch** (NOT hand-rolled memmap2+bincode; the old "usearch vs plain mmap" open decision is CLOSED).
- **exact_search_f32** (NOT HNSW — exact/100% recall, vendor-recommended <1M vectors).
- **load() into a WRITABLE RAM index**, NOT view()/mmap (view() is read-only #97; at ~58MB f32 load() is cheap and keeps the index writable, which incremental needs). load() is scale-bounded — revisit view() past ~20 daemons / 500MB+ monorepo.
- **f32 first** (i8 = TLDR-ccg; separate quantized index + quantized query).
- Metric = cos (unit-normalized vectors).
- Win: 1.4GB JSON -> ~58MB f32 binary; daemon RSS ~2.3GB -> ~0.5GB.

### Sidecar — SCOPE EVOLVED (resolved with Codex review)
Original cold-CLI scope: ALL metadata was rebuilt by chunk_code() at search time, so NO persistent sidecar was needed (verified: cache.rs stored only {vector, cached_at, file_mtime}). That no longer holds. With the warm DAEMON path now shipped (TLDR-atc), the store is PERSISTED and served from a resident daemon, so a restart must serve results WITHOUT re-chunking the whole tree. Therefore a metadata **SIDECAR + MANIFEST** is now a **correctness** requirement of l5d (not optional, not deferrable to t8f). Full design: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (§4 sidecar/manifest, §7 crash-safe save).

### Targets / honesty
- Cold first-load: ~14s (1.4GB JSON) -> sub-second (usearch load()).
- Warm daemon repeat query: ~45ms — ALREADY ACHIEVED via atc + the root-relative cache-key fix.
- Do NOT promise a fixed latency for directory searches; the sidecar now persists metadata so warm dir searches can skip re-chunking (measure before promising).

### PASS/FAIL = results-equivalence, not latency
Exact brute-force + f32 kept -> new ranked results must be IDENTICAL to the JSON-cache path (n=52 eval + captured baselines). Latency improvement is necessary but NOT sufficient.

### Children
l5d (usearch store + sidecar + manifest + crash-safe save), m01 (rewire EmbeddingCache onto the store, keep a content-addressed dedup layer), ccg (i8 compact mode), t8f (incremental delta), zr6 (filtered/scoped search).
design:
RESOLVED design (supersedes the earlier 'plain mmap vs usearch' + 'NO sidecar' framing).

## Bottleneck (measured)
Cold query = 4.3s JSON parse + 0.5s model load + 0.2s query embed + ~0.1ms cosine. The brute-force cosine is irrelevant; HNSW would make it 0.5ms AND approximate. KEEP exact brute-force semantics via usearch exact_search_f32. KEEP f32.

## Store (usearch)
- key (u64) -> vector. add_f32 on build; remove(key) for deltas; exact_search_f32 for query; save/load (writable RAM, not view()).
- One logical store per model (dim fixed per model: arctic-s 384 / m 768 / l 1024).
- Content-addressed DEDUP layer kept SEPARATE from key->vector: a (content_hash, model, embed_schema) -> vector lookup so renames/ordinal-churn re-add without re-embedding (the current EmbeddingCache role; do NOT drop it).

## Sidecar + manifest (l5d correctness — see design doc)
- Sidecar: key -> {identity, file_rel_path, fn, class, line range, content_hash}; per-file record {keys, mtime, size}.
- Manifest covers EVERY input that changes vectors or chunk boundaries: format_version, generation, embedding_model, model_revision (tokenizer+weights), dimensions, metric, scalar_kind, search_mode, embed_schema, chunk_params, walker_version, root, chunk_count, keys_checksum, sidecar_checksum. load() rejects any mismatch -> full rebuild.
- Crash-safe save: generation-suffixed immutable files + a CURRENT pointer whose atomic rename is the sole commit point + dir fsync + keep gen-1 (design doc §7.1). usearch save() is NOT atomic by itself.
- Snippets: read lazily from source at query time, validated by content_hash, degrade gracefully (no sidecar bloat).

## GC
content_hash in the dedup key => changed content orphans old entries. GC-by-reachability on full re-index (drop keys not re-added) OR a small key->timestamp file. (Decide in m01.)

## Verification = results-equivalence
Captured baseline outputs (scores+snippets) + the n=52 eval are the golden reference. Exact brute-force + f32 => IDENTICAL ranked results old vs new. Latency alone is not pass/fail.
notes:
## Baseline Measurements (JSON-based, release build)

Cache: ~/Library/Caches/tldr/embeddings/cache.json = 1.1 GB, 117,641 entries

### Completed Tests

| Test | Command | Wall time | Internal latency_ms | Result |
|------|---------|-----------|---------------------|--------|
| 1. Fresh embed (cached) | `tldr embed config.rs` | 4.36s | 4,393ms | 28 chunks, all cached |
| 2. Cached embed | `tldr embed config.rs` | 4.36s | 4,315ms | 28 chunks, all cached |
| 3. Semantic search (single file) | `tldr semantic "parse json config" config.rs` | 5.22s | 795ms | 10 results, cache_hit=false |
| 5. Cold search (single file) | `tldr semantic "error handling" config.rs` | 7.37s | 1,098ms | 10 results |
| 6. Config model check | `tldr embed config.rs` (config=arctic-l) | 4.41s | 4,370ms | model: arctic-l ✓ |
| 7. CLI flag override | `tldr embed config.rs --model arctic-s` | 4.41s | 4,378ms | model: arctic-s ✓ |
| 8. Existing tests | `cargo test semantic` | 60s (compile) / 6.88s (run) | — | 8 passed, 17 ignored |

### Key observation
Even for a CACHED 28-chunk file, embed takes 4.3s wall time. That's ~4.3s spent loading/parsing the 1.1GB JSON cache, NOT embedding. The actual search latency (795ms-1098ms) is on top of the cache parse.

### Pending Tests (running — first-time arctic-l embed of 14K chunks)
| Test | Command | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| 4. Similar function search | `tldr similar config.rs --function from_path` | Embedding 14,144 chunks (first time arctic-l) |
| 5b. Cold search (full src/) | `tldr semantic "error handling" src/` | Embedding full directory (first time arctic-l) |

These will complete and be recorded. The one-time embed cost is NOT part of the comparison — only the subsequent cached searches matter for the usearch benchmark.
2026-06-02 DECISION RESOLVED + reconciled with Codex review (thread a708b3161b2e63d89). (1) The 'usearch vs plain mmap (memmap2+bincode)' open decision is CLOSED -> usearch library (decision-locked memory: decision-locked-2026-06-01-vector-store-usearch). The hand-rolled memmap2 plan (and TLDR-j8c 'MmapVectorStore') is OBSOLETE. (2) Backend: usearch load() into a WRITABLE RAM index (NOT view()/mmap) — view() is read-only and at ~58MB f32 mmap earns nothing; load() keeps the index writable for deltas. load() is scale-bounded: revisit view() for the read path past ~20 daemons / 500MB+ monorepo. (3) SIDECAR REVISED: this epic previously said 'NO sidecar needed (metadata from re-chunk at search time)'. That holds for a COLD single-shot CLI, but NOT once the store is PERSISTED + served from a resident daemon (TLDR-atc, now done): a restart would have to re-chunk the whole tree to recover result metadata. So a metadata SIDECAR + MANIFEST is now a CORRECTNESS requirement of l5d, not optional. KEEP these still-valid caveats from the original epic text: exact brute-force (not HNSW); f32 first (i8=ccg, separate quantized index); single-file vs directory latency differ — do NOT promise 730ms for directory searches (re-chunk cost stays unless the sidecar persists metadata — which it now does). Full design: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (317293b).

comments.text: BENEFIT-SIZING (2026-06-01, via tldr explain/structure + daemon route scan): The 4.3s JSON load is paid on EVERY semantic query, not once-per-daemon. CONFIRMED: tldr-daemon exposes 21 routes (ping/status/tree/structure/extract/imports/calls/impact/dead/importers/arch/cfg/dfg/slice/complexity/search[BM25]/context/smells/maintainability/secrets/vuln) and ZERO semantic/similar/embed routes — semantic.rs has 0 daemon references. The scan it does NOT help: top_k_similar (similarity.rs:238, cyclo 3, called by SemanticIndex.search:429/find_similar:533) is 0.1ms. usearch benefit = view() mmap kills the 4.3s load on every query + RSS GBs->working-set; exact_search_f32 keeps results identical. title: Wire resolved embedding model into Embedder (provider seam, local-only)
description: Make tldr semantic / tldr embed use the CONFIGURED model instead of the hardcoded default. Resolution order for model: -m flag > config embedding.model > built-in default (arctic-m). parse_model() already maps arctic-xs/s/m/m-long/l (semantic.rs:123) and to_fastembed_model() maps the enum (embedder.rs:187). Provider seam: if embedding.provider != "local", FAIL FAST at load with a clear message ('cloud embedding providers not supported in this build') — fields are still parsed so the file stays forward-valid.
