comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
GATE-1 ROUND-5 PROFILE (measurement ONLY, no code — user directive; 2026-06-05 morning). Repro at HEAD 97b0324, 3 runs: scan ~15ms / parse 2.5-2.7s / compose 19.5-20.7s / total 22.3-23.2s; counts 745/20,326/26,321 (drift from overnight 26,312 = corpus now contains rounds 2-4 code itself). TWO samples (1ms, windows t≈5-19s and t≈14-21s) give IDENTICAL splits — distribution is stationary, not window bias.

WHERE THE ~20s LIVES (% of sampled on-CPU window; sample1/sample2):
- 72%/72.5% — apply_type_resolution -> RustReceiverIndex::lookup -> str::find (StrSearcher::new dominates = setup cost of millions of tiny finds). This is scan_weird (type_resolver.rs:1233): NON-plain-ident receivers (dotted: self.field etc — the majority in Rust) x 3 kinds x 2 patterns x let_lines. Memoized per distinct (receiver,kind), but distinct-receivers x let_lines is the bill, and patterns like 'let self.config: ' are guaranteed-miss outside comments/strings. The three inlined kind-lookup sites sample EQUAL (2857/2817/2739) = receivers falling through ALL THREE kinds, confirming guaranteed-miss traffic. Minor: lookup allocates var_name.to_string() per call for the HashMap probe (type_resolver.rs:1294).
- 21%/20.5% — resolve_local_fuzzy_match (resolution.rs:1527): func_index.iter() FULL SCAN over all ~40k entries PER CALL SITE; profile shows Filter::next + memcmp + std::path Components::eq (the entry.file_path != current_file predicate). The round-1 find_by_name secondary index EXISTS but this site never migrated to it. Round-1's 'real defect, wrong size' is now sized: it was ~4s hiding under the then-65s text scans.
- 4%/3.6% — resolve_type_aware_fallback (resolution.rs:1702): same full func_index.iter() per call site + bare_target.to_string() alloc per methods.contains.
- 0.3% — resolve_global_fuzzy_match: round-1 index working as intended.

CONCLUSION (fact): 97% of sampled compose sits in three NAMED sites, all the same defect class as rounds 1-4 (per-call-site linear work that should be indexed lookups). Nothing sampled looks like inherent global-resolution cost.

HYPOTHESIS (explicitly NOT a result — round-1 lesson): fixing all three should cut compose materially; magnitude unknown until fixed AND re-benched. Candidate fix shapes for the authorization discussion: (a) extend round-3's inverted pass to weird receivers — per let-line, index each 'let '/'let mut ' occurrence remainder keyed by the text preceding ': '/' = ' -> O(occurrences) build, O(1) lookup, output-identical by the same construction argument as rounds 3-4; (b) resolve_local_fuzzy_match -> find_by_name(bare_target) then filter by file; (c) resolve_type_aware_fallback -> find_by_name likewise. All three await user authorization (no-code directive in force).

COST OF THIS ROUND: 3 bench runs (~70s wall), zero rebuilds (overnight unstripped HEAD binary reused), bench-process RAM only. Profiles: /tmp/compose-sample.txt, /tmp/compose-sample2.txt.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
ECONOMICS RE-DERIVATION (queue item 4, 2026-06-05 — honest numbers, conditional verdict; supersedes the 2026-06-04 17:49 benefits statement where they conflict).

TIME AXIS — what chunking actually buys now:
- Measured: parse 2.5-2.7s, compose 19.5-20.7s. Chunks memoize PARSE ONLY; compose is global and re-runs on every edit AND restart regardless of chunking. So chunk persistence buys <=2.7s per rebuild. That is the ENTIRE time-economics of the chunk store unless compose goes incremental (a much bigger design, explicitly out of v1).
- The original 'restart: 3.5min+22GB -> seconds via mmap' claim CONFLATED parse + compose + the (now-fixed) rounds-1-4 defects. Post-overnight, a full callgraph rebuild is ~22s with NO chunk store at all. Restart-from-chunks = compose-only ~19.5s today. Chunk-store restart win = the parse share = ~2.7/22.3 = ~12%.
- Gate-1 verdict: OPEN pending round-5 fixes + re-bench. Scenarios: S1 (compose -> ~2-5s): recompose-always viable, Gate-1 effectively passes — but then full rebuild is ~5-8s total and chunking's time value shrinks to ~2.7s saved = MARGINAL. S2 (compose stays >=10s): per-edit recompose-always dead; lazy compose = 10-20s first-query-after-edit (tolerable-ish for CLI, weak for interactive); incremental compose forced if better is required. NOTE THE SHAPE: in BOTH scenarios the time case for Path B is far weaker than at design time, when the premise was saving ~220s.

MEMORY AXIS — the honesty correction:
- The 27.0GB peak is the EMBED phase (Flow-2, TLDR-3rh): overnight diff touched only callgraph/* + daemon warm-insert; semantic/ ran byte-identical to main. Also the 27.0 came from the getrusage high-water probe (shipped 2 days ago) vs the historical 22.7 from 15s ps polling — a true max SHOULD read above a sampled poll; partly better measurement, not necessarily worse behavior. Post-redesign daemon peak = max(phase peaks) = EMBED. The chunk store does not touch the headline memory number. That peak belongs to TLDR-3rh/yll.
- Callgraph-phase plateau: 11.4GB measured PRE-F1 (with the to_value DOM). F1 is shipped; post-F1 plateau is UNMEASURED (= morning-queue item 2: daemon warm ~22s; awaiting user authorization since it leaves a resident daemon). The chunk store's memory win = whatever transient remains AFTER F1 (+ what surgical F2-F4, TLDR-1in, could take without any storage layer).

NET: BOTH axes moved toward Path A (surgical: F1 shipped + F2-F4 + round-5 compose fixes + naive generation persistence) and away from Path B (full two-flow store) this session. NOT a verdict — two decision inputs still missing: (1) post-round-5 compose number (needs code authorization), (2) post-F1 warm peak (item 2, needs daemon authorization). Make the Path A/B call only when both exist; do not re-lock anything before then.

CAVEAT: all numbers are rust-on-tldr-code. Python/TS/generic resolvers share the defect class (queue item 3) and their compose economics may differ materially per language. What Path B uniquely retains regardless: cross-process/CI chunk sharing (worth ~parse only), mmap-evictable steady-state, restart without parse — re-weigh these against Path A once (1) and (2) land.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
GATE-1 ROUND 5 RESULT — GATE-1 PASSES (2026-06-05 morning, commit follows round-5 profile; user-authorized code round, <1h budget, actual ~35min).

Implemented the three profiled fixes (one shared defect class):
(a) RustReceiverIndex weird path: inverted at construction — per let-line, every candidate (var,kind) enumerable as text-between-prefix-occurrence-and-separator; lookup is now a hash get for dotted receivers too. scan_weird/let_lines/weird_cache deleted; resolve/lookup take &self.
(b) resolve_local_fuzzy_match -> find_by_name (identical multiplicity: one yield per (module,name) alias, argued + gate-verified).
(c) resolve_type_aware_fallback -> find_by_name + alloc-free methods checks.

MEASURED (3 runs, HEAD corpus): scan ~10ms / parse 2.5-2.7s / COMPOSE 0.55-0.56s / total ~3.1s. Round-over-round: compose 19.5s -> 0.56s = 35x this round. CAMPAIGN NET: compose 223s -> 0.56s = ~400x; total build 226s -> 3.1s = ~73x. Parse now dominates (~83% of build).

GATE-1 VERDICT: PASS — compose 0.56s is under the 2s recompose-per-edit line with margin. Recompose-always v1 is RESURRECTED as designed; the escalation ladder (query-time resolution / incremental compose / Glean sets) is NOT needed.

CORRECTNESS EVIDENCE: (1) frozen-corpus (/tmp/tldr-bench @001fdcb) counts BYTE-IDENTICAL to the entire overnight campaign: 745/20,320/26,312; (2) exhaustive differential test extended with 9 weird-receiver stressors (empty receiver, 'mut a', 'a: A', separator-containing vars) — green; (3) 1,053 callgraph tests green; full core+cli suites green except the documented pre-existing TLDR-wks/TLDR-6q2 families (cpp-inheritance x1, explain/taint x36, embed x18 — verified same families, zero new); (4) full 8.4MB edge JSON byte-identical across two runs — output is now DETERMINISTIC (old iter()-order code was per-process random; same nondeterminism class as TLDR-2af).

ECONOMICS DELTA (updates the conditional in the prior comment — scenario S1 realized, stronger than projected): full rebuild ~3.1s, parse-dominated. Chunk-store time value = the 2.7s parse memoization on a 3.1s build; restart-from-chunks ~0.6s vs 3.1s rebuild. Time case for Path B storage is now MARGINAL on rust-on-tldr-code; the live decision inputs are memory (post-F1 warm peak, item 2, still awaiting daemon authorization) + other-language resolvers (queue item 3: python/TS share the per-call-site-scan defect class — their compose numbers unknown).

REMAINING perf note (not blocking, unprofiled): compose 0.56s not yet re-profiled; next marginal candidates if ever needed: per-lookup var_name.to_string() key alloc, enclosing_class_for_call/find_best_vartype per-call scans. Diminishing returns — parse is the new ceiling.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: ROUND-5 VERIFICATION CLOSER (advisor-prompted): the 'byte-identical' claim for fix (a) was count-level on the real corpus + value-level on synthetic input only. Closed: new permanent corpus-level differential gate (rust_receiver_index_matches_legacy_on_real_corpus) — for 400+ REAL Method/Attr call-site receivers extracted from the crate's own callgraph sources, index and legacy scanner agree value-level (catches same-count-different-type divergence). Green. (b)/(c) remain honestly characterized as count-identical + newly-deterministic (legacy iter() order was per-process random, so value-level equivalence is not even well-defined for them).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
DECISION EVOLUTION CHAIN (ADR set, 2026-06-05): the full journey — wrong assumption, premature lock, the gate that caught it, the 400x fix campaign, and the honest re-derivation — is captured as decision records, superseded ones kept deliberately:
- TLDR-cv9 ADR-1 [CLOSED, superseded by ADR-6]: design premised on 'parse expensive, compose cheap' — never measured, measured backwards.
- TLDR-v88 ADR-2 [CLOSED, superseded by ADR-3]: marked architecture LOCKED before Gate-1 ran — the owned process mistake (manufactured false certainty).
- TLDR-k89 ADR-3 [ACTIVE]: nothing above PROPOSED until kill-gates are measured; no storage code before Gate-0/1.
- TLDR-w9z ADR-4 [ACTIVE]: Gate-1 FAIL treated as suspected defect -> profile/fix loop, not architecture escalation (vindicated: 223s -> 0.56s, 7 defects, 5 rounds; lessons: sum ALL branches, re-profile your own fixes).
- TLDR-f0n ADR-5 [ACTIVE]: output-identity discipline — layered differential gates, legacy kept as oracle.
- TLDR-7mp ADR-6 [ACTIVE, supersedes ADR-1]: Gate-1 PASS; recompose-always stands; time case reduced to ~2.7s; Path A/B deferred to post-F1 warm peak + python/TS numbers.
- TLDR-ab6 ADR-7 [ACTIVE]: headline 27GB peak is EMBED phase — memory work routed to 3rh/yll + F-series, not the chunk store.
Point of the chain: not why a stale decision exists, but that the evolution is on the record.

title: F2-F4: structural memory reductions in callgraph builder (AST streaming, index dedup, string interning)
description: F2: build_indices_parallel (builder_v2.rs:638) — drop source text + tree-sitter AST immediately after FileIR extraction; consider bounding rayon width for peak control. F3: unify the duplicated FuncIndex/ClassIndex (builder_v2.rs:682-685 'We need our own copies because the IR's indices use a different format'). F4: intern strings (ustr/lasso) for file paths/names/signatures across nodes, edges, indices. Sequence and prioritize per the Phase-A measurement (TLDR-335) — do the biggest measured contributor first.

title: unify the two divergent load_tldrignore implementations into one shared tldr-core matcher
description: Two implementations exist with DIFFERENT semantics: scanner.rs:529 (Gitignore-based, proper glob/path matching via matched_path_or_any_parents — used by callgraph scan + bugbot) vs warm.rs:323 (crude component-name HashSet, e.g. 'corpus' matches ANY dir named corpus at any depth — used by warm walks + language detection). Same .tldrignore file, two different answers. Consolidate on the Gitignore-based impl, export it from tldr-core (e.g. pub is_tldrignored(root, path, is_dir) or a loaded matcher type), migrate warm.rs callers, delete the HashSet version. Prereq for TLDR-1j2/1qv so all three consumers share one matcher.

title: semantic corpus: enumerate_corpus_files / is_corpus_file ignore .tldrignore — full build over-indexes excluded dirs
description: enumerate_corpus_files (tldr-core chunker.rs:417) and is_corpus_file (chunker.rs:312) honor .gitignore/DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_DIRS/sentinels but never read .tldrignore (zero refs in chunker.rs/walker.rs). So the initial warm SEMANTIC build embeds tldrignored dirs: wasted embed time, inflated build RSS (relevant to TLDR-yll characterization on repos with large ignored dirs), polluted search results — while callgraph scan + warm.rs walks DO honor .tldrignore. Two divergent impls exist: scanner.rs:529 Gitignore-based (correct semantics) vs warm.rs:323 name-stem HashSet (crude). Fix: thread the scanner.rs-style matcher into the chunker walker (WalkBuilder supports add_custom_ignore_filename or explicit Gitignore). NOTE: chunker.rs doc says enumerate_corpus_files is the SINGLE source of truth shared with the freshness gate (TLDR-kkt) — changing membership rules invalidates store freshness digests; expect a one-time full rebuild after fix. Fixing TLDR-1j2 (watcher delta path) alone leaves full-build vs delta-path membership inconsistent.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: Evidence from TLDR-1j2 repro: scratch project warm produced 3 baseline vectors where 2 came from the .tldrignore'd vendored/ dir — initial semantic build over-indexes excluded dirs, confirmed on branch build 2026-06-04.

title: Memory-pressure escape hatch for never-idle daemons (observed 22.7GB RSS during build)
description: Codex-identified gap (risk #2), accepted-trade-off counterweight to TLDR-cxa: presence-based liveness means long-build machines hold a resident daemon (usearch store + ONNX embedder) indefinitely; observed RSS during a full warm build was ~22.7GB at 951% CPU (2026-06-04, tldr-code 1565 files). No memory-pressure state exists in DaemonConfig (types.rs:42) and no memory fields in FullStatus (types.rs:519). SCOPE: (1) investigate/characterize build-time RSS first — 22.7GB may itself be a bug (HYPOTHESIS per Codex, not confirmed); (2) add RSS to daemon status; (3) policy decision: optional max-RSS config that aborts/defers builds or allows idle-out under system memory pressure, as an explicit OPT-IN that overrides the presence philosophy. Parallel after A.

title: MCP liveness parity — tldr_mcp binary must also defer daemon idle shutdown
description: Codex-identified gap (risk #1): a CLI-wide poke in run_command (main.rs:584/619) does NOT cover MCP — crates/tldr-cli/src/bin/tldr_mcp.rs is a separate binary entrypoint. An agent using ONLY MCP tools still reproduces the original TLDR-3w5 bug (daemon dies under it). Fix: the MCP server sends the same registry-gated datagram poke per tool call (or, once TLDR-utj.5 lands a real MCP daemon client, its socket traffic covers it naturally — coordinate with utj.5 to avoid double-building). Parallel after A's tracker API is stable.
close_reason: Shipped: sender moved to tldr_core::liveness (single source for registry path + poke path + datagram send; CLI delegates), MCP handle_tools_call pokes before dispatch. Tests: core ancestor-match e2e, MCP tools/call integration test with bound datagram receiver. Note for TLDR-utj.5: when the real MCP daemon client lands, its socket traffic adds Socket presence but the per-call poke stays (covers non-routed tools), mirroring the CLI.

title: CLI-wide liveness poke: every tldr invocation defers daemon idle shutdown (registry-gated unix datagram)
description: Child B of TLDR-cxa (Codex verdict: NEEDS-CHANGE, changes folded in). Today only ~18/64 commands touch the daemon socket; ~45 generate zero liveness. IMPLEMENT IN run_command TOP-LEVEL DISPATCH (main.rs:619) — NOT daemon_router (opt-in via try_daemon_route, daemon_router.rs:51, covers only 12 commands). TRANSPORT (Codex): registry-gated UNIX DATAGRAM side channel — NOT stream connect-and-close (stream connect can block up to 5s, ipc.rs:397; accepted odd connects update activity then log 'Connection error', daemon.rs:321/328) and NOT touch-file (turns the 100ms accept loop, daemon.rs:316, into a perpetual disk poller). GATE: read_registry (prunes dead entries, daemon_registry.rs:165; entry carries project/pid/socket, daemon_registry.rs:41) — no daemon registered => no syscall beyond one small file read. CONSTRAINTS: zero perceptible latency on unrelated commands (tldr loc must not get slower), no retries, silent failure, opt-out env var for CI/bulk. Depends on A's presence API; independent of C.
close_reason: Shipped: registry-gated unix datagram poke in run_command (all surfaces), ancestor project matching, TLDR_NO_POKE opt-out, daemon-side receiver touching CliPoke, cleanup parity via PokeGuard + cleanup_socket_at sibling removal. Verified live: non-routed command pokes, subdir pokes, zero latency delta, no orphans. macOS unbound-datagram semantics verified empirically before building.

title: daemon status: liveness + build observability (per-source last-activity, busy+age, build progress, idle deadline)

During a 90-min build there is NO way to distinguish building from done except poking semantic. COMPAT (Codex P3): response decoding is untagged with no versioning scheme visible in types.rs — adding fields must preserve decode order for older clients. Depends on A (TLDR-3w5) defining the tracker state this reports.

title: tldr embed: surface legacy-builder notice — steer callers to daemon start + warm flow
description: Found while testing the Phase 1 flow. 'tldr embed' is the legacy FOREGROUND builder: it silently runs the full (potentially ~90min) embedding build in the caller's terminal and exercises none of the new daemon warm path. Nothing in its output or help tells the caller that the blessed flow is now 'tldr daemon start && tldr warm'. Per the two-modes philosophy (works beautifully or says why), decide and implement ONE of: (a) KEEP embed as an explicit offline/CI builder but print a prominent notice on every invocation + in --help ('legacy foreground builder — prefer tldr daemon start && tldr warm; embeddings are shared via the per-chunk cache either way'); or (b) PARK it with the standardized message 'not available in this version, <reason>' like similar/--hybrid. Lean (a): embed writes the same per-chunk embedding cache that warm reuses, so it has legitimate offline/CI value — but it must say what it is. ACCEPTANCE: calling tldr embed surfaces the guidance; --help updated; scripts/smoke-test-cli.sh 'embed' case expectation updated to match the chosen behavior.

title: global -q flag swallows entire JSON result for 'cache stats' / 'daemon status|list|notify' (stats unaffected)

'tldr -q cache stats' and 'tldr -q daemon status' exit 0 with EMPTY stdout, while 'tldr -q stats' correctly prints its JSON result. -q should suppress progress chatter only, never the result payload. Repro: 'tldr -q daemon status | wc -c' -> 0; 'tldr daemon status | wc -c' -> 65. Suspect these commands print results via the progress/eprintln-gated path instead of the output.rs result path.

title: tldr warm client times out at 30s while daemon build continues — misleading 'Failed to send' error
description: Observed during TLDR-7xz smoke test: 'tldr warm .' against a daemon whose semantic build exceeds 30s prints 'Error: Failed to send warm command to daemon: connection timeout after 30s' — but the command WAS delivered and the daemon keeps building (subsequent queries correctly say 'index build in progress'). The message reads as failure while the build is succeeding. Fix options: (a) daemon ACKs warm immediately and builds async, with 'tldr daemon status' exposing build state; (b) longer/streaming client wait with progress. Option (a) fits the serve-or-say-why philosophy best. Parked under the Phase 2 epic.
close_reason: Shipped: Warm handler spawns detached WarmJob + immediate ack (started/already_building), busy guard pre-ack, single-flight latch with panic-safe clear, model resolved at ack time, client relays the poll-status message. Verified live: 29ms ack, honest duplicate answer, full build lifecycle observable in daemon status, idle deadline restarted on completion.

title: (EPIC) Phase 2: raise the ceiling — warm everything + retrieval quality
description: Phase 2 un-parks everything Phase 1 honestly parked, and raises quality. Research-grounded (firecrawl, 2026): the SOTA shape is two-stage hybrid retrieve (dense + BM25, RRF) -> rerank. The dominant quality lever is the MODEL: current ArcticL is general BERT; code-specialized decoder models (voyage-code-3 / jina-code-embeddings / Nomic CodeRankEmbed / CodeSage-v2) substantially outperform. title: Notify pipeline: debounce/coalesce + burst-cap on the delta path (§6)
description:
P2 of the incremental-reindex rollout. t8f landed the SYNCHRONOUS delta core (one delta per Notify, on the existing dirty_files set). This issue adds the §6 debounce/coalesce pipeline so bursts of saves collapse into one batched delta job and a tree-wide churn flips to a full rebuild.

SCOPE (design doc §6):
- Debounce: per-file quiet timer (default 750ms) that re-arms on each accepted Notify.
- Hard max-wait deadline (default 5s from the FIRST event in a batch) so a steady low-rate stream can't starve the quiet timer forever.
- On flush: drain the pending set and run the §5 delta PER FILE inside ONE spawn_blocking job (handle_notify stays lock-free on the async thread — TLDR-qr9).
- Coalescing: N saves to one file -> one delta; saves across N files -> N deltas in one job.
- Burst cap (rolling): schedule a SINGLE full rebuild instead of deltas when pending > 200 files OR >1000 accepted events in a 2s window; the rebuild supersedes queued deltas (clear pending). All three numbers are config knobs.

NOTE: the source-files-only filter (§6 first gate) is split out as TLDR-ac0.6 (P1, correctness) and should land first. This issue is the perf/coalescing layer on top. Overlaps TLDR-ac0.2 (in-daemon watcher rewriting handle_notify to a channel) — coordinate so the debounce lives on the channel side, not duplicated.
acceptance_criteria: A burst of N rapid Notifies to one file produces exactly one delta job; a steady low-rate stream still flushes within max-wait; >200-file or >1000-event/2s bursts trigger a full rebuild instead of per-file deltas (pending cleared); handle_notify never blocks the async runtime; debounce params are config-driven.
notes: Design §6. P1=delta core (t8f, DONE) -> P2=this (debounce) -> P3=incremental persistence (separate). Built on the dirty_files HashSet + auto_reindex_threshold already in daemon.rs.

title: IndexManager: resident Embedder reuse (avoid per-query ONNX reload)
description: query_store() calls Embedder::new(model) per query (store_search.rs:192), which reloads the ONNX model every search. The daemon's resident IndexManager should hold (or pool) a warm Embedder alongside the VectorStore so the hot path reuses it. Requires changing query_store's signature to accept a pre-built Embedder. Flagged during ac0.1 (Codex review note: 'warm Embedder REUSE, no per-query construction').
close_reason:
Resident-embedder reuse on the query path landed. query_store() split into query_store_with_vector() (takes a pre-computed query vector); daemon IndexManager::query() now embeds via the SAME resident embedder field (embed_query -> Arctic query prefix), BEFORE taking the store lock, preserving parallel warm store.search (ac0.1). Empty-query guard lives in query_store_with_vector (the fn the daemon calls directly). No per-query Embedder::new/ONNX reload on the warm path.

VERIFIED (results-equivalence): new #[ignore] parity test resident_embedder_query_matches_fresh_embedder_ranking — cold fresh-embedder path vs warm resident-embedder path produce IDENTICAL rankings (passing, 2.70s).

MODEL-SELECTION AUDIT (per user directive 'no hardcoded model, use config'): swept crates for EmbeddingModel::Arctic*. Production code is CLEAN — no hardcoded model selection (only the required enum->fastembed match in embedder.rs + an example bench). All real embedding/search paths resolve via EmbeddingModel::resolve(cli, TldrConfig). Aligned tests to the same path: new parity test + the two pre-existing #[ignore] store_search.rs tests (search_with_store_rebuilds_persists_and_loads, store_freshness_gate_rebuilds_only_on_source_drift) now resolve the model from config instead of hardcoding ArcticXS — all pass on ArcticM.

Pre-existing exhaustive_matrix.rs failures filed as TLDR-wks (independent).

title: Spike: Qdrant (embedded mode) as vector-store backend — glue-deletion assessment
description: Spike Qdrant as a replacement for the usearch+sidecar store. Qdrant normally runs as a SERVER (gRPC/HTTP) — evaluate whether its embedded/in-process Rust crate path is viable for an offline CLI. Qdrant carries rich payload storage + filtering that overlaps our glue, but the server-first architecture is a direct tension with HARD CONSTRAINT #2 (no separate process).
design: Check vs the 4 HARD CONSTRAINTS in parent TLDR-e9j. Constraint #2 (embeddable, no server) is the likely disqualifier — assess FIRST and bail early if Qdrant cannot run in-process. If it can, payload + filter API would absorb the meta sidecar and scoped-query glue (TLDR-zr6).
acceptance_criteria: Report: (a) is there a genuinely EMBEDDED in-process mode (no daemon/server), or does it require spawning a Qdrant process? If server-only -> NO-GO and stop; (b) exact-search support for constraint #1; (c) glue absorbed + net LOC deleted; (d) dependency/build cost; (e) GO/NO-GO.
close_reason: Won't do: user committed to usearch+glue (2026-06-02). Qdrant Edge IS a real in-process Rust crate (overturned the server-only assumption) but irrelevant given the decision to stay on usearch.

title: Spike: LanceDB as vector-store backend — glue-deletion assessment
description: Spike LanceDB (embedded, Rust-native, columnar Lance format) as a replacement for the usearch+sidecar store. LanceDB stores vectors AND arbitrary columns (metadata) in one table with built-in persistence and predicate filtering — directly overlapping the glue we hand-wrote. Most promising candidate because it is embeddable (no server) and Rust-native.
design: Check vs the 4 HARD CONSTRAINTS in parent TLDR-e9j. KEY RISK: LanceDB's vector search may be ANN-first (IVF/HNSW); confirm a brute-force/exact path exists, else it fails constraint #1. Metadata-as-columns would delete the key->ChunkMeta HashMap sidecar AND the save/load manifest. Filtering (TLDR-zr6 scoped queries) may come for free.
acceptance_criteria: Report: (a) does LanceDB support EXACT search (not just ANN/IVF-PQ) so the TLDR-2a4 equivalence harness still passes? (b) which glue it absorbs — meta sidecar / persistence / filtering / freshness; (c) net LOC deleted from vector_store.rs; (d) new dependency weight + build cost; (e) GO/NO-GO.
close_reason: Won't do: user committed to retaining usearch+glue + fully in-memory index (2026-06-02). LanceDB research (firecrawl) recorded the finding (exact kNN via bypass_vector_index, BYO-vectors boundary = Arrow RecordBatch, heavy arrow-58 dep tree, less-mature Rust SDK) but the decision is to stay on usearch. No migration spike needed.

title: Re-evaluate vector-store backend vs embedded vector DBs (code-reduction lens)
description: The TLDR-uwb backend decision only weighed library-INDEX options (usearch, FAISS, HNSW) against a HAND-ROLLED mmap store. It never considered the embedded vector-DB tier (LanceDB, Qdrant, Chroma, Milvus), which bundles metadata + persistence + filtering and could absorb the ~1,100 non-test lines of glue in vector_store.rs. This is the umbrella to evaluate that blind spot. GOAL: decide whether a higher-level lib lets us DELETE store glue without breaking the hard constraints. Children spike LanceDB and Qdrant against a shared rubric.
design: HARD CONSTRAINTS any candidate must preserve (these are why usearch won): (1) EXACT search determinism — results must match the old scan bit-for-bit (TLDR-2a4 equivalence harness must still pass); (2) EMBEDDABLE in the CLI binary — NO mandatory separate server process; (3) LOCAL-ONLY / offline — no network, no API key at query time; (4) f32 exact mode (no forced quantization). EVALUATION RUBRIC (score each candidate): how many of these glue concerns does it absorb? — key->ChunkMeta sidecar, save/load manifest+integrity, corpus-digest freshness gate, capacity growth, u64 collision guard, path/lang filtering. Net glue-LOC delta. Build/CI cost (vs usearch's accepted C++/cxx cost). Maturity/maintenance.
acceptance_criteria: A written recommendation (keep usearch+glue, or migrate) backed by the per-candidate spikes, with a glue-LOC-deleted estimate for each, and TLDR-uwb amended or superseded accordingly.
close_reason: Won't do: backend re-evaluation concluded by user decision (2026-06-02) to RETAIN usearch+glue and keep the index fully in-memory. The blind spot TLDR-uwb missed (embedded vector DBs) was researched: LanceDB (GO-capable, exact kNN, but heavy arrow deps + Arrow-RecordBatch glue + less-mature Rust SDK) and Qdrant Edge (real in-process crate). Decision: not worth the dependency weight + determinism re-validation to delete ~1100 lines of glue. usearch+glue stands.

title: elixir structure method_infos/methods extraction broken (2 failing tests)
description: PRE-EXISTING, unrelated to m01/kkt/o48/4oz/lx6 (git diff df97ff3..HEAD shows my commits never touched elixir/structure code). Two tests in crates/tldr-cli/tests/elixir_method_infos_v1.rs fail: test_structure_elixir_method_infos_populated and test_structure_elixir_method_infos_count_matches_methods. They build an .ex fixture, run 'tldr structure', and expect files[0].methods[] to contain bar/baz and method_infos to be populated/count-matched. Currently failing -> elixir method extraction in the structure command is broken or its output schema drifted. Surfaced during the lx6 full-suite verification (earlier incomplete streaming captures of 'cargo test --workspace' hid it). These are the LAST 2 known default-suite failures after o48/4oz/lx6 fixed. Investigate the elixir structure/method extraction path.
notes: 2026-06-02 ROOT CAUSE PINPOINTED (diagnosis, not yet fixed). The failing tests (crates/tldr-cli/tests/elixir_method_infos_v1.rs) panic at line 66 (.expect methods array present) — the legacy 'methods' field is ABSENT (empty + skip_serializing_if). Ran tldr structure on the fixture: classes=['Foo'] OK, method_infos=[bar,baz] OK, but BOTH functions=None AND methods=None. So: method_infos works (built from extract_definitions -> try_elixir_call_definition, a GENERIC full-tree walk that matches a call's first 'identifier' child == def/defp; extractor.rs:234). The legacy methods (extractor.rs:218) comes from module_info.classes[].methods via extract.rs extract_elixir_module_info -> extract_elixir_module_functions (extract.rs ~6896/module fns). functions comes from extract_elixir_functions_detailed (extract.rs:6697). BOTH of those use POSITIONAL navigation (child.kind()=='call' then child.child(0) text=='def') and BOTH return empty, while the generic walker succeeds — strongly implies the tree-sitter-elixir parse shape no longer has 'def' at child.child(0) where the positional extractors look. CANDIDATE FIX: make extract_elixir_functions_detailed + extract_elixir_module_functions match the robust 'first identifier child' pattern that try_elixir_call_definition uses (NOT child.child(0)). REGRESSION GUARD: extractor.rs:205-209 invariant — sum(functions)+sum(methods) must equal health.summary.functions_analyzed; fixing functions extraction changes that sum, so re-verify the health invariant + the canonical-function-enumerator tests. Needs parse-tree confirmation before editing.
close_reason: FIXED (test-correction, not extractor). Root cause: the 2 tests asserted on the JSON 'methods' field, but methods AND functions are intentionally serde(skip_serializing) (types.rs:1276/1289, schema-cleanup-v1 BUG-13) — never in JSON for ANY language; method_infos is the sanctioned serialized surface. Elixir extraction was NOT broken: instrumented extract_from_tree returns functions=[bar,baz] and class Foo methods=[bar,baz] correctly, and method_infos JSON already had both with correct lines/signatures. NOTE: a Codex investigation proposed an extractor rewrite, but that was wrong for this symptom — it would not make the tests pass (panic is on get(methods), absent due to skip_serializing). Fix: updated the tests to assert on method_infos (names contain bar/baz; count==2) instead of the unserialized methods field; updated docstrings. 2 passed. Uncommitted in working tree.

title: clones_output_is_byte_stable flakes/fails: non-deterministic clone output
description: PRE-EXISTING (reproduced at commit 8a19b09, BEFORE the o48/4oz/warnings work — not a regression from it). crates/tldr-cli/tests/determinism_and_stderr_hygiene_v1.rs:230 clones_output_is_byte_stable runs 'tldr clones' 3x (stripping detection_time_ms) and asserts byte-identical JSON; it FAILS consistently (3/3) -> clone_pairs[] or some other field is still non-deterministically ordered despite the prior stability fix. Earlier workspace baseline runs MISSED this because the streaming 'cargo test --workspace' output capture was incomplete (it under-counted total passed). This is the 3rd known pre-existing default-suite failure alongside the now-fixed o48/4oz. Investigate the clones detector output ordering (HashMap iteration?) and make it deterministic; then the default 'cargo test --workspace' is fully green.
close_reason: FIXED (test fix). The clone CONTENT was deterministic all along; clones_output_is_byte_stable only stripped stats.detection_time_ms (+ a non-existent 'metadata' key) and MISSED summary.detection_time_ms, so the unstripped wall-clock timing made the byte comparison fail. Now strips detection_time_ms recursively. 5/5 green. Stale test, not a product non-determinism bug.

title: find_similar path matching fails on path-form mismatch (pre-existing; breaks tldr similar + ignored test)
description: PRE-EXISTING (reproduced on df97ff3, BEFORE any m01 work — NOT caused by TLDR-m01). The model-gated test semantic::index::index_tests::semantic_index_find_similar fails with ChunkNotFound { file: 'config.py', function: Some('parse_config') }. ROOT CAUSE: SemanticIndex::find_similar (index.rs:~518) matches the query chunk with an EXACT full-string compare c.chunk.file_path.to_string_lossy() == file_path. The test builds the index from temp_dir.path() so chunks carry the ABSOLUTE path (/tmp/.../config.py), but passes the BARE 'config.py' -> never matches. This is masked because the test is #[ignore]'d (loads the ONNX model), so default cargo test never runs it. find_similar is a PRODUCTION path: crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/similar.rs:158 (tldr similar <file> --function <fn>), so the same exact-match fragility likely breaks or surprises real CLI usage whenever the user-supplied path form differs from how chunks stored it (relative vs absolute vs canonical). FIX OPTIONS: (a) match by suffix/normalized path (ends_with on normalized separators, like the eval/store root_relative), or (b) canonicalize both sides before compare, or (c) fix the test to pass the stored path form AND harden find_similar for the CLI. Decide whether find_similar's contract takes a root-relative or absolute path and make CLI + matching consistent. Out of scope for m01; file kept separate.
close_reason: FIXED (commit 1db1c08). SemanticIndex::find_similar now matches the caller's path against chunk paths via canonicalize_for_match (exact fast path -> canonical-form fallback), so 'tldr similar' works when the caller's path form (canonicalized in similar.rs) differs from the walker's stored chunk path. Self-exclusion now keys off the query chunk's OWN stored path. Stale test fixed to pass a real path; added find_similar_matches_despite_path_form_mismatch (covers the '/./' form-mismatch via the canonical fallback). Both find_similar tests pass with the model.

title: GC reader-grace contract for the view()/mmap query path
description: Codex re-review (TLDR-l5d) #11: VectorStore::load() now takes a SHARED lock (vs save's EXCLUSIVE) so the RAM load path is safe against in-flight saves/GC. But the read-only view()/mmap QUERY path (TLDR-m01 rewire) does NOT yet hold that lock — a reader that mmaps index.<gen>.usearch can have it GC'd out from under it by a concurrent save. Need a reader-lock-or-retry contract: reader snapshots CURRENT, opens the immutable trio under a shared lock OR retries on a GC'd/missing file. Couple this with KEEP_GENS + the 60s grace window so a slow reader's gen survives. Blocks safe concurrent daemon query + reindex.
notes: 2026-06-02 REFRAMED by the fully-in-memory decision (memory in-memory-index-decision-2026-06-02). pdb's claim 'blocks safe concurrent daemon query+reindex' is SUPERSEDED by TLDR-ac0.1 (Arc<RwLock<VectorStore>> handles daemon concurrency in RAM — no per-query mmap). pdb now narrows to the COLD-CLI mmap-reader vs daemon-save GC race ONLY, and becomes fully MOOT if the cold CLI also load()s into RAM. Do not implement as written; re-scope after ac0.1.

title: Deterministic store tie-break for reproducible search output (l5d follow-up)
description: From the l5d n=52 equivalence review (REAL=0 verified): the usearch store's tie order for equal-cosine duplicate chunks is usearch-internal, and the dense path's tie order is filesystem-walk-order — neither is stable/reproducible across machines. OPTIONS: (A) accept usearch-native order (zero cost, default today); (B) post-sort store search hits by (distance asc, key asc) -> fully reproducible, hash-stable order independent of usearch internals, ~1 line, RECOMMENDED for test/output determinism; (C) strict byte-parity to the dense path via f64 rerank of usearch top-(K+margin) + a stored insertion-ordinal tie-break (matches an arbitrary walk-order baseline, costs holding/fetching vectors + ordinal). DECISION PENDING (user was in hold/discuss). Not a correctness blocker (REAL=0). Recommend B unless a consumer needs exact dense-order parity. Relates to l5d/ac0/m01.

title: DECISION: ArcticL retained as deployed default; embedding-model SIZE is not the quality lever
description: MEASURED (clean baseline, n=14, prefix ON): ArcticL (1024d) R@5 0.786/R@10 0.929/MRR 0.707 vs ArcticM (768d) R@5 0.857/R@10 0.857/MRR 0.685. The M-vs-L difference is 1-2 single-case swings = WITHIN NOISE at n=14; the models are statistically indistinguishable on this eval. DECISION: (1) RETAIN ArcticL as the deployed default (user accepts the ~3x index time / 33% larger vectors; it is a hair better @10/MRR, not worse). (2) Treat embedding-model SIZE as NOT a quality lever for code search — the bottleneck is the MODALITY GAP (English query vs wordless code), already addressed by the query prefix (12x). Future quality gains come from TLDR-4er (lexical fusion) and TLDR-9m2 (LLM descriptions), NOT a bigger encoder. (3) Do not re-chase 'use a larger embedding model' — it has been measured and it does not move recall. Revisit only with the expanded gold set (TLDR-6h3), since n=14 cannot resolve model deltas anyway.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR, 2026-06-01). Model-size measured and ruled out as a quality lever; ArcticL retained as deployed default. Recorded so future sessions don't re-chase a bigger encoder.

title: Opt-in i8 'compact mode' quantization (4x smaller index, near-lossless for cosine)
description: usearch ScalarKind::I8 quantization: ~98.9% Recall@1 (near-lossless), valid for cosine (our metric), and our embeddings are ALREADY unit-normalized (matches usearch's i8 normalize->[-127,127] process). Shrinks the on-disk/mmap'd index ~4x (1.1GB->~280MB f32->i8; b1 binary even smaller) -> faster view() load, faster exact_search, lower RSS. OPT-IN only (config 'compact'/flag): i8 reorders vs f32 so it breaks the v1 results-equivalence gate (TLDR-2a4) — must be measured against the eval harness before recommending. Reframes closed TLDR-8pt as a usearch-native option.
acceptance_criteria: A config/flag enables i8 storage; eval harness shows recall delta vs f32 within an accepted bound; index size and cold-query latency drop measurably; f32 remains default.
notes: 2026-06-02: i8 is NOT just a flag (Codex review). exact_search_f32 gets no i8 benefit — i8 needs a SEPARATELY built quantized index (add_i8 / ScalarKind::I8) AND the QUERY quantized to i8 (exact_search_i8). Near-lossless for cosine on unit-normalized vectors, ~4x smaller. Gate: measure recall on the n=52 eval before flipping the default. Manifest scalar_kind already distinguishes f32/i8 stores (design doc §4.0).

title: Scoped semantic queries via usearch filtered_search (path/language predicate)
description: Today 'tldr semantic <q> <subdir>' re-chunks + re-embeds the subdir each call (cold). With a single GLOBAL usearch index, usearch filtered_search_f32 + a StatefulPredicate can restrict results to a path prefix or language AT QUERY TIME — no per-scope re-embed. The metadata sidecar (key->{path,lines,lang}) supplies the predicate inputs. Big win for scoped/interactive queries once the global index + daemon warm path exist. Composable predicates (path AND language).
acceptance_criteria: tldr semantic <q> <subdir> filters a single global index by path predicate (no subdir re-embed); language filter works; results identical to filtering the unscoped results post-hoc.

title: DECISION: Arctic query prefix is ON by default (the recall fix)
description: MEASURED (eval, ArcticM, crates/tldr-core/src, 14 gold): embedding raw code without Arctic's query prefix gives near-random recall (recall@5 0.071). Prepending the trained query prefix ('Represent this sentence for searching relevant passages: ') to the QUERY only (not passages) lifts recall@5 to 0.857 / MRR to 0.685 — a 12x gain. DECISION: prefix is DEFAULT-ON (shipped e9e5427, TLDR-dlk closed). It is the model's trained-correct mode; do not regress. Caveat captured separately: it lowers absolute cosine scores -> the 0.50 CLI threshold must be recalibrated (TLDR-h27).
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: DECISION: v1 keeps f32 (exact-equivalence); i8 quantization is an opt-in 'compact mode', not default
description: usearch ScalarKind::I8 quantization is near-lossless for our case (98.9% Recall@1; valid for cosine; our embeddings are ALREADY unit-normalized — exactly usearch's i8 process) and 4x smaller (1.1GB->~280MB) + faster load/search/RSS. BUT i8 reorders results vs f32, which would break the results-equivalence PASS/FAIL gate (TLDR-2a4). DECISION: v1 ships f32 to preserve exact equivalence with the current brute-force baseline. i8 becomes an OPT-IN 'compact mode' (config/flag) AFTER v1 lands and equivalence is verified — measured against the eval harness, not assumed. (Reframes the closed TLDR-8pt as a usearch-native opt-in.)
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: Embed existing docstrings to bridge the modality gap (free, no-API-key precursor to 9m2)
description: Rust enrichment docstring field is a stub (always empty), so the 'Description:' line is omitted from embedding text vs the Python original. Populate docstrings (ast layer) for full recipe parity. Low priority; enrichment works without it.
notes: REFRAME (2026-06-01) — UPGRADED from P3, status understanding corrected. Earlier dismissed as low-value 'because enrichment is off'. WRONG framing: enrichment was gated off due to the CFG/DFG FLOW noise hurting recall — docstrings are a DIFFERENT component. Docstrings are English text the author already wrote = directly bridges the MODALITY GAP (English query vs wordless code), the root problem. So d8f is a FREE, no-API-key cousin of TLDR-9m2: embed existing docstrings to lift recall for documented functions, WITHOUT the CFG/DFG flow info. Decouple from the recall-hurting enrichment: add a docstring-only embedding-text path (gate separately from TLDR_ENRICH). Measure on the eval. Likely cheaper first step than 9m2 (which LLM-generates descriptions for functions that LACK docstrings).

title: Detection pass: annotate semantic/search code with audit findings
description: Embed the session's investigation into the code as grep-able // TLDR-AUDIT(<id>): comments so the next session can start from the code itself. Non-functional: comments only, no logic changes. Marks dead code (cs5 stub, 4er hybrid), regressions (7kf scan, k4q JSON, 8pt f32), and the misnamed flag (1a8).
close_reason: Annotated 7 files with 11 grep-able // TLDR-AUDIT(<id>): comments capturing the investigation (cs5 dead stub, 4er unwired hybrid, 7kf/k4q/8pt regressions, 1a8 misnomer, 6fm root-cause). cargo check passes, 0 errors. Comments only, no logic changes.

title: Promote semantic search to a first-class architectural layer (Layer 6: Retrieval) + document it
description: Root cause of this epic (per docs/ARCHITECTURE.md v2.0): the canonical architecture is a 5-layer STATIC stack (AST->CallGraph->CFG->DFG->PDG). Semantic/embeddings/BM25/hybrid are NOT a layer, NOT in the data-flow diagram, NOT in the caching section — they exist only as throwaway CLI lines (search.rs/semantic.rs) and one MCP wrapper. That non-adoption is WHY the subsystem rotted (FAISS-downgrade, dead HTTP stub, unwired hybrid) while the documented layers stayed clean. This issue is the umbrella architectural work that the six sibling fixes serve: elevate semantic search to a first-class layer with the same rigor as the static layers.
design: Deliverable: (a) a real Retrieval layer in tldr-core with documented key types, owning embedding + index + hybrid fusion; (b) an ARCHITECTURE.md section for Layer 6 (purpose, key types, data-flow position, caching) — the doc work has no other home; (c) the in-process usearch index as the layer's persistence/search engine. MUST honor the documented 'intentionally avoids LSP' principle: in-process only, no HTTP service, no Redis. Caching should fit the documented daemon in-memory LRU model (metadata sidecar parsed once into daemon memory).
acceptance_criteria: ARCHITECTURE.md documents Layer 6 (Semantic/Retrieval) with the same structure as Layers 1-5; semantic search is reachable as a coherent tldr-core module rather than scattered CLI/MCP glue; the 6 sibling fixes (7kf/8pt/k4q/4er/cs5/1a8) land under this layer.
notes: SESSION UPDATE (2026-06-01): Scope grew. 'Layer 6: Retrieval' documentation must now also cover the mmap persistence engine from epic TLDR-ac0 (the decided storage layer) and the query-prefix recipe (TLDR-dlk). The Layer-6 ARCHITECTURE.md section should describe: chunker -> embedder (with Arctic query/passage prefix) -> mmap vector store -> brute-force top_k -> optional RRF hybrid (4er). Best done LAST, after 4er/ac0 land so the doc matches reality.

title: EmbeddingClient HTTP path is a no-op stub
description: embedding_client.rs:197 .search() returns Ok(Vec::new()) — the HTTP embedding service path does nothing and silently returns empty. FIX: implement the HTTP call, or delete the path entirely. Currently a trap for anyone who wires it in expecting results.
notes: SESSION UPDATE (2026-06-01): Still accurate — EmbeddingClient HTTP path is a no-op stub. Now has concrete urgency: it is the blocker for TLDR-4er, whose value is no longer hypothetical (dense-alone misses 2/14 lexically-strong gold cases that BM25 fusion should recover). Fix = delete the stub + repoint 4er's semantic side at in-process SemanticIndex. Per epic NOTES, the 8765 HTTP service this stub implied NEVER existed in either codebase.
close_reason: Dead EmbeddingClient HTTP stub DELETED (search/embedding_client.rs + types.rs placeholder + mod/lib re-exports + tests). hybrid_search decoupled from any embedding backend — now takes &[SemanticResult] supplied by the caller. Full workspace builds green.

title: RRF hybrid fusion is dead code (unwired)
description: search/hybrid.rs implements BM25+dense Reciprocal Rank Fusion but is referenced NOWHERE in the live CLI (grep confirms zero hits outside archived/). Production gives EITHER lexical (tldr search/BM25) OR dense (tldr semantic), never fused. FIX: wire enriched_search / tldr semantic to call fuse_rrf so the hybrid path actually runs.
design: This is the highest-value item: the most modern retrieval idea in the repo never executes.
notes: SESSION UPDATE (2026-06-01): Concrete value evidence now exists. Dense-alone (prefix ON) misses exactly 2/14 gold cases — 'extract a control flow graph from a function' (cfg/extractor.rs) and 'build the enriched text...' (enrichment.rs) — both LEXICALLY strong (a fn literally named extract_cfg*), precisely where BM25 fusion should recover them. So 4er is not just 'completeness'; it has a measurable recall target (could lift 0.857 toward 1.0). IMPL SKETCH (read this session): repoint hybrid_search's semantic side from the dead EmbeddingClient (TLDR-cs5) to the in-process SemanticIndex; aggregate chunk-level dense hits to FILE granularity (BM25 Bm25Result keys by file, RRF fuses on file path string); handle #[cfg(feature=semantic)] gating (hybrid.rs is currently un-gated in search/); wire hybrid_search into the CLI (today it is called nowhere outside the crate). Blocked on cs5.
close_reason: RRF hybrid fusion WIRED end-to-end. hybrid_search decoupled from the dead stub (cs5); new hybrid_search_with_index builds dense from in-process SemanticIndex, reduces to best-per-file, RRF-fuses with BM25. Wired into BOTH consumers: 'tldr semantic --hybrid' CLI + MCP tldr_semantic tool (was BM25-only). Fixed a path-key bug (dense=cwd-relative vs BM25=root-relative -> overlap always 0; now strip root so fusion actually combines). MEASURED (ArcticL, prefix ON, n=14): dense->hybrid Recall@5 0.786->0.929, Recall@10 0.929->1.000, MRR 0.718->0.783; cfg/extractor.rs recovered (— -> rank 8). Eval measures the exact production path. Caveat: file-level fusion; n=14 (see 6h3). Full build green, 31/31 search tests pass.

title: Vector search uses brute-force scalar scan (no SIMD/ANN)
description: similarity.rs:237 does an O(n) linear scan with a plain scalar cosine loop on every query. Defensible at the 10K-100K target scale (sub-ms, exact), but leaves a 5-10x constant-factor win on the table and the 100K ceiling reaches ~10ms/query. REPLACE: SimSIMD kernel dropped into top_k_similar for the cheap win; usearch (HNSW) only if scale ever exceeds ~100K vectors.
design: Swap surface is exactly one function: top_k_similar in semantic/similarity.rs. No design change needed for SimSIMD; usearch would require an index build/persist step.
notes: SESSION/DESIGN UPDATE (2026-06-01): CHALLENGED BY MEASUREMENT (epic TLDR-ac0). ac0 measured the cold-query breakdown: 4300ms JSON parse + 500ms model load + 200ms query embed + 0.1ms brute-force cosine. The scalar scan THIS issue targets is the 0.1ms term — NOT the bottleneck. ac0 keeps exact brute-force (HNSW/usearch is approximate AND only saves ~0.1ms->0.5ms). Likely SUPERSEDED by ac0 once the mmap-vs-usearch fork (TLDR-rgc) is locked. Do NOT implement SIMD/ANN until rgc decides. The old handoff framing 'usearch subsumes 7kf' is itself stale — ac0 argues AGAINST usearch.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by usearch decision (TLDR-rgc, 2026-06-01). usearch exact_search_f32 owns the vector scan — exact, SIMD-accelerated in C++. Also moot per ac0 measurement (scan was 0.1ms, never the bottleneck). No custom SIMD/ANN needed.

title: Semantic search: modernize vector lookup + complete hybrid retrieval
description: Umbrella for improving tldr's semantic/search subsystem. Two themes: (1) the vector-lookup mechanics are naive (brute-force scalar scan, f32, JSON persistence) though defensible at current scale; (2) the most modern retrieval design in the tree (RRF hybrid fusion) is built but unwired, and an alternate embedding path is a stub. Children track each problem and replacement. We will pick each child and discuss before implementing.
design:
FRAMING: Perf items (scan/quant/persist) are REGRESSIONS from the Python original (FAISS), not net-new gaps. Completeness items (dead RRF path, stub client, misnamed flag) are the higher-value fixes. The rewrite as a whole was net-positive (better modular architecture, in-process Arctic embeddings); it only regressed the vector-INDEX layer and left two features half-built.

DECIDED DIRECTION: Finish the in-process path with usearch (the Rust FAISS-equivalent that closes the FAISS-shaped hole WITHOUT a service dependency). Do NOT revive the HTTP embedding service (re-introduces cross-language/operational fragility the author was rightly avoiding). Do NOT add Redis (external server; the daemon already provides warm in-memory). 

usearch SUBSUMES three children: its Rust binding (usearch/rust/lib.rs) provides save/load + view (mmap) + ScalarKind quantization (F32/BF16/I8/B1x8) natively. So 7kf (scan), 8pt (quant), k4q (persistence) collapse into 'adopt usearch' + one small residual task: a metadata sidecar (u64 key -> {path,lines,snippet,hash,mtime}) that usearch does NOT store. JSON sidecar is acceptable — daemon parses it once and holds it in memory, so format has zero query-time cost.
notes:
PYTHON-PRECEDENT (llm-tldr/tldr/semantic.py): Python was MORE sophisticated at vector search — in-process BGE-large-en-v1.5 (sentence-transformers, 1024d) + FAISS IndexFlatIP + binary .faiss persistence (semantic.py:1068,1072,1134). Rust kept in-process embeddings (good, Arctic 768d) but DOWNGRADED the index: FAISS->scalar loop, binary->JSON. So 7kf/k4q are regressions, not net-new.

THE 8765 SERVICE NEVER EXISTED — ANYWHERE. Grep of the Python repo: no FastAPI/Flask/uvicorn/8765/endpoints. Python embedded in-process (semantic.py:206). So embedding_client.rs (cs5) was a NEW planned architecture, not a wrap of existing Python. The BGE-1024 model ref shows it was meant to run the same engine as Python, as a separate service.

AUTHOR DESIGN INTENT (reconstructed): strangler-fig migration — defer the hard ML port (sentence-transformers+FAISS are hard in Rust) by keeping embeddings in a Python HTTP service and calling it from Rust during the transition. The 'Mitigation M8 graceful-degradation -> BM25-only' header comment proves they knew the dependency was fragile. Then they pivoted to in-process fastembed (correct call) but only finished HALF: embeddings came in-process, FAISS's job was NOT replaced (no Rust ANN lib reached for) -> hand-rolled scalar scan. Result: dead HTTP stub + regressed index. usearch is the library that finishes the pivot.

8765 IS NOT LSP AND NOT MCP — it is a 4th orphaned subsystem. Transports proven: daemon=Unix socket / TCP axum (port=hash-of-project, NOT 8765); MCP=stdio JSON-RPC (no port); diagnostics 'LSP' tools=subprocess+parse stdout (NOT real LSP sessions; only severity enum is 'LSP-compatible'); embedding_client=HTTP REST (dead). NOTE: MCP is a CONSUMER of semantic — tldr_semantic is an exposed MCP tool (docs/MCP.md:107) — so the dead embedding layer degrades an agent-facing capability that is already wired up. That raises the value of completing 4er.

ROOT CAUSE — SEMANTIC SEARCH WAS NEVER ARCHITECTURALLY ADOPTED (docs/ARCHITECTURE.md v2.0). The canonical architecture is a 5-layer STATIC stack: AST -> Call Graph -> CFG -> DFG -> PDG. Semantic/embeddings/BM25/hybrid are NOT a layer, NOT in the data-flow diagram, NOT in the caching section — they appear only as two throwaway CLI lines (search.rs/semantic.rs) and one MCP wrapper. This explains WHY the subsystem rotted: the 5 documented layers stayed clean/tested while search — never first-class — got FAISS-downgraded, left with a dead stub and an unwired hybrid path. REFRAME: this epic is not just bug-cleanup; it is PROMOTING semantic search to a first-class architectural layer (Layer 6: Semantic/Retrieval) with the same rigor as the static layers. Deliverable should include: (a) a real Retrieval layer in tldr-core, (b) an ARCHITECTURE.md section documenting it (layer purpose, key types, data-flow, caching), (c) the in-process usearch index as its persistence/search engine.

DESIGN-PRINCIPLE FIT: ARCHITECTURE.md states 'intentionally avoids LSP' for speed/token-efficiency. usearch (in-process, no server) HONORS that principle; reviving the HTTP service or adding Redis would VIOLATE it. The documented caching model (daemon in-memory LRU keyed by tool+args+mtimes) also matches the proposed JSON metadata sidecar (parsed once into daemon memory) — so the recommended direction is consistent with the intended architecture, not a deviation.

MODEL NOTE: Python used BGE-large (1024d); Rust uses Arctic (768d). Deliberate swap (Arctic newer), but index dimensions differ from the Python era — not a parity port.

DOC DRIFT: ARCHITECTURE.md says 18 languages (overview) but the tier table lists 17; CLAUDE.md context implies ~15. Language count not pinned consistently — sign the docs are loosely maintained.

comments.author: Udhayakumar

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: CONSOLIDATED PROBLEM STATEMENT (2026-06-01). The semantic subsystem has TWO ORTHOGONAL axes that this epic (quality/completeness) and TLDR-ac0 (perf/storage) split between them. ROOT PROBLEM = the MODALITY GAP: queries are English, raw code lacks those words -> embedding raw code gave ~random recall (R@5 0.071). QUALITY levers, ranked by MEASURED impact: (1) Arctic query prefix = DONE, 12x (->0.857); (2) BM25+dense RRF fusion (TLDR-4er, future); (3) LLM function descriptions (TLDR-9m2, future, biggest remaining lever); (4) model SIZE = NOT a lever (ArcticM 768 vs ArcticL 1024 are indistinguishable at n=14 — within single-case noise). BASELINE (deployed ArcticL, prefix ON, n=14): R@5 0.786 / R@10 0.929 / MRR 0.707 / ~92min cold. Persistent miss on BOTH models: cfg/extractor.rs. Small wins shipped: h27 (threshold->0.0 makes recall visible), y0q (eval uses deployed model), 1a8 (--hybrid->--filter). The eval's n=14 is too small to trust deltas -> gold-set expansion filed.

comments.author: Udhayakumar

comments.text:
DE-STALE 2026-06-03: The 2026-06-01 staleness-flag comment is itself now stale on two points:
(a) It says ac0 'replaces only the persistence layer with a plain mmap store (explicitly AGAINST usearch ... + C++ dep)' and 'the mmap-vs-usearch fork is the open decision rgc.' RESOLVED: usearch was CHOSEN (DECISION uwb; rgc CLOSED; hand-rolled mmap / j8c obsolete). ac0 = usearch exact_search_f32 + load() into writable RAM (3il/zim). ac0 DOES keep brute-force + f32 (that part of the flag still holds) — but it is NOT 'against usearch.'

Perf children 7kf/8pt/k4q CLOSED (subsumed by ac0). Epic now 11/14 (78%).

title: Tests + docs for usearch store migration
description: Update cache.rs unit tests for the new store (open/get/contains/add/flush, corruption, version mismatch, concurrent access). Update CLAUDE.md / build notes: confirm NO new C++ toolchain dependency (pure-Rust path), document the per-model store files and the GC/compaction command. Ensure cargo test -p tldr-core --features semantic and tldr-cli semantic tests pass (note: pre-existing PoisonError flakiness in tldr-cli semantic tests is tracked separately).
notes: REFRAMED mmap->usearch.

title: Migrate/discard cache.json -> usearch index
description: Handle the existing 1.1GB cache.json (~/Library/Caches/tldr/embeddings/cache.json, 117,641 entries) gracefully -- must NOT crash on first run with old cache present (smoke test #9). Options: (a) one-time import that reads cache.json once and writes per-model store files, then deletes/renames the JSON; (b) ignore old cache and lazily rebuild on demand (slower first runs but simplest). Decide and implement; ensure no data-loss surprise for users mid-upgrade.
notes: REFRAMED. The 1.1GB cache.json is already dead (raw-v1 key bump orphaned the 117K old entries). Simplest path: drop it, rebuild the usearch index from chunks on next run (re-embed once). No row-by-row migration needed unless we want to preserve current raw-v1 vectors.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: POST-ZXB (2026-06-02): The query path no longer touches cache.json (VectorStore is the only search path per TLDR-lx7). The JSON cache is ONLY used inside VectorStore::build as the content-hash dedup layer. 3gj scope is now clear: move dedup into VectorStore itself (a content-hash->vector sidecar) and delete EmbeddingCache/cache.json entirely. This eliminates the 4.3s JSON deserialize even on rebuilds. The existing 1.1GB cache.json can be dropped — vectors are rebuilt from chunks on first run of the new path (re-embed once).

title: GC-by-reachability for orphaned vectors
description: On a full-root re-index, enumerate all live keys (from chunk_code over the whole project) and drop store entries not re-added this pass -- reclaims orphans from deleted/renamed/changed code. Single-file embeds can't see the whole project, so orphans accumulate until a full pass; expose a compaction trigger (e.g. tldr embed --gc or tldr index --compact). Content-hash keys already guarantee correctness, so this is space reclamation only.
notes: REFRAMED: GC = rebuild the usearch index from live chunks on full re-index (per TLDR-rgc GC decision). Orphaned vectors simply don't get re-added. Drops per-entry timestamps; matches the rebuild model.

title: spike: oxc_resolver as library replacement for hand-rolled JS/TS module resolution in compose
description: Library-first follow-up from research annex v2 (TLDR-zde). oxc_resolver (Rust enhanced-resolve port, used by Rspack/Rolldown, handles tsconfig paths/exports maps/monorepo aliases) could replace builder_v2's hand-rolled ModuleIndex/ImportResolver for JS/TS — including the VAL-007 workspace-alias logic. Compatibility spike: does its resolution semantics match builder_v2's contract for tldr's supported languages? Post-v1; do not block the analysis store.

title: Cross-session persistence: inject project decisions/context at session start (no cold start)
description:
From GrapeRoot competitive analysis (TLDR-2o4), finding #4.

PROBLEM: GrapeRoot persists decisions and 'WHY' memory across sessions and injects them at startup — no cold start. tldr has nothing; this repo papers over it with beads/bd remember, but that's a personal setup, not a tldr feature.

PROPOSAL: persist a distilled session digest (hot files/symbols, decisions surfaced) in the project cache dir; SessionStart hook injects it. Builds on session memory (TLDR-2o4.2). SCOPE CAUTION: decision/WHY capture borders on issue-tracker territory (beads) — keep tldr's slice to CODE context continuity (hot set, recent structural changes), not task memory.

title: Live session cost visibility: queryable mid-session token/cost stats
description:
From GrapeRoot competitive analysis (TLDR-2o4), finding #6.

PROBLEM: our telemetry measures tldr's OWN savings (justifies tldr) but gives the user no session-cost observability. GrapeRoot has a passive dashboard and answers 'what's my cost so far?' mid-session.

PROPOSAL: daemon-side session token ledger (fed by hooks' session-end/usage events where available) + 'tldr session stats' command + expose via MCP so the agent itself can answer cost questions. Lowest priority of the set — nice-to-have vs the architecture gaps. Note overlap with existing savings telemetry (TLDR daemon stats) — extend, don't duplicate.

title: ignore-file mid-session reload: watcher rebuilds matchers when .tldrignore/.gitignore change
description: v1 of TLDR-1j2 loads matchers once at spawn_watcher; editing .gitignore/.tldrignore mid-session requires a daemon restart (documented limitation). Enhancement: the watcher already receives events for those two root files — on change, rebuild the matchers (swap behind ArcSwap/RwLock in the debounce-handler closure). Low priority; do after 1j2 lands.

title: idle_timeout semantics migration: client-idle -> project-presence-idle (config naming + docs)
description: Codex-identified gap (risk #4): idle_timeout_secs is documented as client 'idle timeout' (types.rs:52). TLDR-cxa changes its meaning to project-presence idle — existing user configs silently change behavior (a 1800s timeout that used to reap daemons on busy-build machines no longer will). SCOPE: decide naming (keep key + redefine vs new key + deprecation), update config docs + DAEMON_SEMANTIC_ARCHITECTURE.md §11, and emit a one-line startup log stating the effective liveness policy so the change is visible. Lands with or immediately after A.
close_reason: Shipped: keep-key-redefine decision documented in types.rs field doc; startup log line states effective liveness policy; §11.2b in architecture doc documents presence sources + retired workarounds; TROUBLESHOOTING/daemon.md purged of fictional --idle-timeout flag and stale 300s claim.

title: pre-existing test failures found during TLDR-7xz verification (not regressions)
description: All verified to fail identically on base tree (git stash) on 2026-06-04: (1) med_cleanup_bundle_v1::m14_smells_notes_deep_only_analyzers — expects a --deep notice on stderr that no longer appears; (2) bench_patterns_security_multilang::test_inheritance_cpp_unsupported — asserts C++ inheritance returns 0 classes but the module now extracts 3 (stale assertion after C++ support landed); (3) exhaustive_matrix explain/taint x18 — 'function_name' vs 'function' schema drift, already tracked as TLDR-wks (commented); (4) exhaustive_matrix embed x18 — test_embed_on_c HANGs >30s (debug ONNX first load) then poisons embedding_mutex cascading PoisonError to 17 cells (also in TLDR-wks comment); (5) schema_cleanup_v1 x10 — requires /tmp/repos/flask fixture, absent on this machine.

title: (Optional) LLM-description enrichment for the modality gap
description: Per the semantic baseline memory: LLM descriptions bridge the code-vs-English modality gap — the biggest remaining recall lever after the model swap (e.g. the persistent 'extract a control flow graph' miss). Evaluate after model swap; may be partially obsoleted by code-specialized models trained on NL2Code.

title: daemon notify: keep as git-hook poke + explanatory comments
description: DECISION (user, 2026-06-03): KEEP — the git-hook poke use case is wanted. It already funnels into the one invalidation path (handle_notify -> process_dirty_file, same as the watcher worker; never a parallel mechanism). Add comments at the CLI command + IPC Notify + handle_notify explaining: this is the git-hook's pipe into the single invalidation/re-index flow; the watcher is the primary source; if the poke use case ever dies, delete the whole chain.
close_reason: Notify kept as git-hook poke: explanatory comments added at all three legs (notify.rs module doc, DaemonCommand::Notify in types.rs, handle_notify in daemon.rs) stating it funnels into the single process_dirty_file flow shared with the watcher, and to delete the whole chain if the use case dies.

title: ac0.5 test-coverage follow-ups (Codex review deferrals)
description:
Two low-priority test-hardening items deferred from the TLDR-ac0.5 Codex review (the substantive finding — blank-query guard before embed — was fixed in commit 7f6dc53):

1) End-to-end blank-query coverage through DaemonCommand::Semantic (socket dispatch), not just IndexManager::query. Current test blank_query_short_circuits_before_building_embedder covers the IndexManager::query method directly (Codex offered 'or', so the guard IS tested); this would add the full daemon command path.

2) Wall-clock watchdog on concurrent_warm_searches_overlap (index_manager.rs) so a regressed lock invariant FAILS fast instead of hanging the suite. Deferred because Rust's harness has no native per-test timeout and the pre-existing concurrent_read_locks_overlap (ac0.1) carries the identical exposure — adding a bespoke watchdog to only the new test would be inconsistent. If done, apply to both via a shared helper.

Parent: ac0.5 (closed). Pure test hardening; no production code change.

title: [bug] exhaustive_matrix.rs explain/contracts tests assert stale field names (binary emits different JSON keys)
description:
Discovered while verifying TLDR-ac0.5. The exhaustive_matrix.rs integration matrix has ~90 failing cases on a CLEAN baseline (verified by stashing ac0.5 and re-running test_explain_on_rust — fails identically). Two distinct causes:

1) FIELD-NAME DRIFT (deterministic, real bug): test_explain_on_* (exhaustive_matrix.rs:1137) asserts json.get('function_name') but the explain command now emits 'function' (e.g. {"function":"main",...}). The test is out of sync with the binary's JSON schema. Likely affects other check_* helpers too. Fix: align the test's expected field names with the binary's actual output (or vice-versa if the schema regressed).

2) ONNX TIMEOUT (environmental): test_embed_on_*/test_semantic_on_*/test_similar_on_* (cfg(feature=semantic)) shell out to the binary and HANG at the 30s wall-clock guard (exhaustive_matrix.rs:231) when the model load/store build exceeds 30s on a cold cache. NOTE: ac0.5's in-process parity test runs the SAME query_store path (cold arm = search_with_store->query_store) in 2.70s, so query_store does NOT hang — the subprocess timeout is model-cache/store-build cost in the harness, not a code regression.

Pre-existing, independent of ac0.5.

title: tldr-mcp cache::bench_cache_hit_latency is a flaky asserting microbench — convert to non-asserting
description:
tldr-mcp cache::tests::bench_cache_hit_latency asserts a raw HashMap lookup stays under 1us (panics with 'Cache hit too slow'). Observed failing at 1.099us during the TLDR-ac0.6 full-workspace test run on a busy machine — a timing artifact, not a real regression (all 44 functional tldr-mcp tests passed). An absolute wall-clock threshold in a unit test is inherently load-dependent and makes 'cargo test' nondeterministic on CI / loaded dev machines.

FIX: drop the hard assertion — either (a) move it behind #[ignore] / a bench harness (criterion or cargo bench) so it measures without gating cargo test, or (b) keep it as a smoke test that prints latency but only asserts a loose ceiling (e.g. <50us) that machine load can't trip. Location: crates/tldr-mcp/src/cache.rs:390. Discovered during TLDR-ac0.6 (2026-06-03); unrelated to that change (no tldr-mcp edits).

title: Non-portable dogfood test: test_e2e_dogfood_no_crash hardcodes /Users/cosimo path
description: crates/tldr-cli/tests/bugbot_check_test.rs line 564 hardcodes codebase = /Users/cosimo/.opc-dev/opc/packages/tldr-code/tldr-rs-v2-canonical, the original authors home dir. On any other machine, tldr bugbot check on that missing dir returns ENOENT, exit is non-zero, and the success assertion fails. The test is ignore-marked and labeled environment-dependent, so it only runs under --ignored, but it is effectively dead for everyone except that machine. NOT a regression; surfaced by a full --ignored run 2026-06-02.
acceptance_criteria: Test targets a portable path such as the CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR env or the workspace root, so it dogfoods tldr on tldr own Rust source and passes on any machine; the existing assertions still hold.
close_reason: FIXED. Replaced hardcoded /Users/cosimo/... path with env!(CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR) so the ignored dogfood test runs portably on this crate's own Rust source. Verified: test passes under --ignored (1 passed, 102s, real bugbot check). Uncommitted in working tree.

title: Fix stale !Sync + freshness doc comments (compiler-disproven)
description: Corrected 6 stale doc comments disproven by compiler-enforced Send+Sync assertions (memory concurrency-sync-evidence-2026-06-02): vector_store.rs:91, index.rs:161, daemon.rs:122, store_search.rs:103, store_search.rs:502, embedder.rs:72. All claimed types are Send+Sync; comments now note the &mut-self method nuance accurately. Comments-only, zero behavior change. cargo check -p tldr-core -p tldr-cli --features semantic passes. Codex-reviewed before + after (after-review caught :502; sweep caught embedder.rs:72).
close_reason: Done — 6 comments corrected, compiles clean, Codex signed off. Uncommitted in working tree (user controls commit/push).

title: Trim hand-rolled store glue via small crates (xxhash-rust + tempfile)
description: Reduce the ~1100 non-test lines of glue in vector_store.rs using small maintained crates (researched 2026-06-02, user committed to usearch+glue, NOT a backend swap). CANDIDATES: (1) xxhash-rust (xxh3) to replace the hand-rolled FNV-1a stable_hash + digest_bytes checksums + identity-key hashing — stable/portable, ~10x MD5. NOTE: rustc-hash/FxHash (already a dep) is NOT stable across releases, which is why FNV was hand-rolled; xxh3 is the correct stable swap. (2) tempfile (NamedTempFile::persist) to replace the save() temp+rename+CURRENT-pointer atomic dance (~80 lines). NOTE: notify-debouncer-full/notify land via TLDR-ac0.2, not here.
design: Swapping the hash = STORE_FORMAT_VERSION 3->4 (old stores rebuild once — cheap). Keep a checksum (xxh3 or crc32fast) for torn-write integrity. Pure glue/perf cleanup; zero behavior change for search results.
acceptance_criteria: Hand-rolled FNV + atomic-write code replaced by crates; persisted store still loads (or a clean STORE_FORMAT_VERSION bump triggers a one-time rebuild); net LOC reduced; equivalence harness (TLDR-2a4) still green.

title: find_similar canonical fallback does O(n) canonicalize syscalls on exact-miss
description: Codex review of TLDR-4oz (commit 1db1c08, Concern #2): SemanticIndex::find_similar's canonical fallback (index.rs) canonicalizes EACH chunk path via std::fs::canonicalize when the exact-string match misses. In production similar.rs passes a canonicalized path while chunks carry the walker form, so the exact pass often misses -> O(n) canonicalize syscalls per find_similar. BOUNDED in practice (similar.rs scopes the index to the queried file's PARENT dir via effective_path, so n is small, not the 100k cap), so NOT urgent. If find_similar is ever run interactively over a large index, precompute/cache each chunk's canonical path at build time (or store chunks canonical) so the fallback is O(1) per chunk. Low priority.
close_reason: FIXED. find_similar's canonical fallback now memoizes canonicalize_for_match by path string (HashMap), so it canonicalizes once per UNIQUE file instead of once per chunk — O(files) syscalls not O(chunks). Behavior-preserving (pure memoization). Verified: both find_similar tests pass under --ignored incl. the path-form-mismatch fallback test. Uncommitted in working tree.

title: cli test_structure_json_output asserts stale 'functions' key (schema is 'definitions')
description: PRE-EXISTING, unrelated to m01 (tldr-cli, not touched by m01). crates/tldr-cli/tests/cli_tests.rs:80 test_structure_json_output runs 'tldr structure <dir> -l python -q' and asserts stdout contains "functions" AND "classes". The structure command's per-file JSON schema uses 'definitions' (FileStructure.definitions: Vec<DefinitionInfo>, types.rs:1320) + 'classes' — there is NO top-level 'functions' key. So the test fails on the stale 'functions' expectation; the product output is correct. FIX: update the assertion to "definitions" (and keep "classes"). NOTE: a sibling test at cli_basic_tests.rs:370 has the same name — check it too. Surfaced during pre-kkt baseline (cargo test --workspace: 1979 pass, this is 1 of 2 known fails).
close_reason: FIXED (commit 1db1c08). cli_tests.rs test_structure_json_output now asserts the actual 'definitions' key (the structure JSON groups function-level items under definitions + classes; no top-level 'functions'). Passes.

title: 'tldr search --hybrid' flag is misnamed (BM25+regex, not dense fusion)
description: search.rs:88 SearchMode::Hybrid means 'BM25 + regex pattern filter', NOT dense+lexical hybrid. Misleading given a real RRF hybrid exists in the tree. FIX: rename the flag (e.g. --filter), or repoint it at the real RRF hybrid once wired.
close_reason: Renamed misleading CLI flag --hybrid -> --filter (it is BM25 + regex post-filter, NOT lexical+dense fusion). --hybrid reserved for real RRF fusion (TLDR-4er). search.rs.

title: Embedding cache persisted as JSON (ASCII floats)
description: cache.rs serializes embeddings as JSON text; the whole file is deserialized into RAM each run. REPLACE: binary format (bincode/rkyv) or memory-mapped file. Faster load, smaller disk, no parse cost.
notes: SESSION/DESIGN UPDATE (2026-06-01): SUBSUMED by epic TLDR-ac0 — replacing the JSON cache (1.1GB ASCII floats, 4.3s parse) IS the whole point of ac0 (mmap store). Superseded by ac0 children TLDR-m01 (rewire EmbeddingCache onto MmapVectorStore) + TLDR-3gj (migrate cache.json). Keep open only as a pointer until ac0 lands. CONFIRMED this session: the e9e5427 cache-key bump (raw-v1 schema tag) orphaned the old 117K model-only-keyed entries -> ~1.1GB DEAD weight now sitting in cache.json, sharpening the urgency.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by usearch decision (TLDR-rgc). usearch save/load + view (binary mmap) replaces the JSON cache — kills the 4.3s parse. Old 1.1GB cache.json is dead weight, discarded on migration (TLDR-3gj).

title: Embeddings stored as full f32, no quantization
description: EmbeddedChunk stores Vec<f32> (~3KB/vector, 357MB at the 100K cap; types.rs:73). REPLACE: scalar/int8 quantization (or usearch built-in i8/b1) to cut memory 2-8x and speed the scan.
notes: SESSION/DESIGN UPDATE (2026-06-01): DEPRIORITIZED by epic TLDR-ac0. ac0 keeps f32 — quantization (f16/i8) reorders brute-force results, and the cold-query cost is the cache LOAD (4.3s) not vector width. Likely superseded/deferred once TLDR-rgc locks the backend. Don't quantize unless a measured memory/size problem reopens it.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by usearch decision (TLDR-rgc). Quantization is built into usearch via ScalarKind (add_f32/f16/i8/b1x8) — opt-in if a size problem ever arises. No custom quant code needed.

title: [Future] Cloud embedding provider behind config seam
description: NOT now — placeholder so the seam is tracked. When implementing cloud embeddings: introduce an EmbeddingProvider trait (embed_batch) with FastEmbedProvider (local, current Embedder) and a RemoteProvider impl. The existing search/embedding_client.rs is a NON-FUNCTIONAL stub (HTTP body unimplemented — comments say 'would use reqwest'; second placeholder unit-struct EmbeddingClient in types.rs:2938; not reachable from CLI). Reuse its request/response shapes + graceful-degradation idea (M8 -> BM25 fallback). Work: add reqwest, real POST to endpoint, auth header from env var named by api_key_env (never store the key), carry dimensions from config (cloud dims differ, e.g. OpenAI 3-large=3072; cache/index need model->dim). Cache already keys by model so cloud embeddings cache cleanly. Flag: cloud means sending code to a third party (privacy). Depends on the config seam landing first.
